name: Chandi Di Vaar
description: Chandi Di Vaar from Sri Dasam Granth Sahib

verses.gurmukhi: ਚੰਡੀ ਦੀ ਵਾਰ ॥
verses.transliteration: cha(n)ddee dhee vaar ||
verses.translation: NAME OF THE BANI.

verses.gurmukhi: ੴ ਵਾਹਿਗੁਰੂ ਜੀ ਕੀ ਫਤਹ ॥
verses.transliteration: ikOankaar vaahiguroo jee kee fateh ||
verses.translation: There is one god and Victory is his.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸ੍ਰੀ ਭਗਉਤੀ ਜੀ ਸਹਾਇ ॥
verses.transliteration: sree bhagautee jee sahai ||
verses.translation: May SRI BHAGAUTI JI (The Sword) be Helpful.

verses.gurmukhi: ਵਾਰ ਸ੍ਰੀ ਭਗਉਤੀ ਜੀ ਕੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: vaar sree bhagautee jee kee ||
verses.translation: The Heroic Poem of Sri Bhagauti Ji

verses.transliteration: paatisaahee 10 ||
verses.translation: (By) TheTenth King (Guru).

verses.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: pauRee ||
verses.translation: PAURI

verses.gurmukhi: ਪ੍ਰਿਥਮ ਭਗੌਤੀ ਸਿਮਰਿ ਕੈ ਗੁਰ ਨਾਨਕ ਲਈਂ ਧਿਆਇ ॥
verses.transliteration: piratham bhagauatee simar kai gur naanak liee(n) dhiaai ||
verses.translation: In the beginning I remember Bhagauti, the Lord (Whose symbol is the sword and then I remember Guru Nanak.

verses.gurmukhi: ਫਿਰ ਅੰਗਦ ਗੁਰ ਤੇ ਅਮਰਦਾਸੁ ਰਾਮਦਾਸੈ ਹੋਈਂ ਸਹਾਇ ॥
verses.transliteration: fir a(n)gadh gur te amaradhaas raamadhaasai hoiee(n) sahai ||
verses.translation: Then I remember Guru Angad, Guru Amar Das and Guru Ram Das, may they be helpful to me.

verses.gurmukhi: ਅਰਜਨ ਹਰਿਗੋਬਿੰਦ ਨੋ ਸਿਮਰੌ ਸ੍ਰੀ ਹਰਿਰਾਇ ॥
verses.transliteration: arajan harigobi(n)dh no simarau sree harirai ||
verses.translation: Then I remember Guru Arjan, Guru Hargobind and Guru Har Rai.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸ੍ਰੀ ਹਰਿਕ੍ਰਿਸ਼ਨ ਧਿਆਈਐ ਜਿਸੁ ਡਿਠੇ ਸਭਿ ਦੁਖਿ ਜਾਇ ॥
verses.transliteration: sree harikirashan dhiaaieeaai jis ddiThe sabh dhukh jai ||
verses.translation: (After them) I remember Guru Sri Har Kishan, by whose sight all the sufferings vanish.

verses.gurmukhi: ਤੇਗ ਬਹਾਦਰ ਸਿਮਰਿਐ ਘਰਿ ਨਉ ਨਿਧਿ ਆਵੈ ਧਾਇ ॥
verses.transliteration: teg bahaadhar simariaai ghar nau nidh aavai dhai ||
verses.translation: Then I do remember Guru Tegh Bahadur, though whose Grace the nine treasures come running to my house.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸਭ ਥਾਈਂ ਹੋਇ ਸਹਾਇ ॥੧॥
verses.transliteration: sabh thaiee(n) hoi sahai ||1||
verses.translation: May they be helpful to me everywhere.1.

verses.gurmukhi: ਖੰਡਾ ਪ੍ਰਿਥਮੈ ਸਾਜ ਕੈ ਜਿਨ ਸਭ ਸੈਸਾਰੁ ਉਪਾਇਆ ॥
verses.transliteration: kha(n)ddaa pirathamai saaj kai jin sabh saisaar upaiaa ||
verses.translation: At first the Lord created the double-edged sword and then He created the whole world.

verses.gurmukhi: ਬ੍ਰਹਮਾ ਬਿਸਨੁ ਮਹੇਸ ਸਾਜਿ ਕੁਦਰਤਿ ਦਾ ਖੇਲੁ ਰਚਾਇ ਬਣਾਇਆ ॥
verses.transliteration: brahamaa bisan mahes saaj kudharat dhaa khel rachai banaiaa ||
verses.translation: He created Brahma, Vishnu and Shiva and then created the play of Nature.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸਿੰਧੁ ਪਰਬਤ ਮੇਦਨੀ ਬਿਨੁ ਥੰਮ੍ਹਾ ਗਗਨਿ ਰਹਾਇਆ ॥
verses.transliteration: si(n)dh parabat medhanee bin tha(n)mhaa gagan rahaiaa ||
verses.translation: He created the oceans, mountains and the earth made the sky stable without columns.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸਿਰਜੇ ਦਾਨੋ ਦੇਵਤੇ ਤਿਨ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਬਾਦੁ ਰਚਾਇਆ ॥
verses.transliteration: siraje dhaano dhevate tin a(n)dhar baadh rachaiaa ||
verses.translation: He created the demons and gods and caused strife between them.

verses.gurmukhi: ਤੈ ਹੀ ਦੁਰਗਾ ਸਾਜਿ ਕੈ ਦੈਤਾ ਦਾ ਨਾਸੁ ਕਰਾਇਆ ॥
verses.transliteration: tai hee dhuragaa saaj kai dhaitaa dhaa naas karaiaa ||
verses.translation: O Lord! By creating Durga, Thou hast caused the destruction of demons.

verses.gurmukhi: ਤੈਥੋਂ ਹੀ ਬਲੁ ਰਾਮ ਲੈ ਨਾਲ ਬਾਣਾ ਦਹਸਿਰੁ ਘਾਇਆ ॥
verses.transliteration: taitho(n) hee bal raam lai naal baanaa dhahasir ghaiaa ||
verses.translation: Rama received power from Thee and he killed the ten-headed Ravana with arrows.

verses.gurmukhi: ਤੈਥੋਂ ਹੀ ਬਲੁ ਕ੍ਰਿਸਨ ਲੈ ਕੰਸੁ ਕੇਸੀ ਪਕੜਿ ਗਿਰਾਇਆ ॥
verses.transliteration: taitho(n) hee bal kirasan lai ka(n)s kesee pakaR giraiaa ||
verses.translation: Krishna received power from Thee and he threw down Kansa by catching his hair.

verses.gurmukhi: ਬਡੇ ਬਡੇ ਮੁਨਿ ਦੇਵਤੇ ਕਈ ਜੁਗ ਤਿਨੀ ਤਨੁ ਤਾਇਆ ॥
verses.transliteration: badde badde mun dhevate kiee jug tinee tan taiaa ||
verses.translation: The great sages and gods, even practising great austerities for several ages

verses.gurmukhi: ਕਿਨੀ ਤੇਰਾ ਅੰਤੁ ਨ ਪਾਇਆ ॥੨॥
verses.transliteration: kinee teraa a(n)t na paiaa ||2||
verses.translation: None could know Thy end.2.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸਾਧੂ ਸਤਜੁਗੁ ਬੀਤਿਆ ਅਧ ਸੀਲੀ ਤ੍ਰੇਤਾ ਆਇਆ ॥
verses.transliteration: saadhoo satajug beetiaa adh seelee tretaa aaiaa ||
verses.translation: The saintly Satyuga (the age of Truth) passed away and the Treta age of semi-righteousness came.

verses.gurmukhi: ਨੱਚੀ ਕਲ ਸਰੋਸਰੀ ਕਲ ਨਾਰਦ ਡਉਰੂ ਵਾਇਆ ॥
verses.transliteration: na'chee kal sarosaree kal naaradh ddauroo vaiaa ||
verses.translation: The discord danced over all the heads and Kal and Narad sounded their tabor.

verses.gurmukhi: ਅਭਿਮਾਨੁ ਉਤਾਰਨ ਦੇਵਤਿਆਂ ਮਹਿਖਾਸੁਰ ਸੁੰਭ ਉਪਾਇਆ ॥
verses.transliteration: abhimaan utaaran dhevatiaa(n) mahikhaasur su(n)bh upaiaa ||
verses.translation: Mahishasura and Sumbh were created for removing the pride of the gods.

verses.gurmukhi: ਜੀਤਿ ਲਏ ਤਿਨਿ ਦੇਵਤੇ ਤਿਹ ਲੋਕੀ ਰਾਜੁ ਕਮਾਇਆ ॥
verses.transliteration: jeet le tin dhevate teh lokee raaj kamaiaa ||
verses.translation: They conquered the gods and ruled over the three worlds.

verses.gurmukhi: ਵੱਡਾ ਬੀਰ ਅਖਾਇ ਕੈ ਸਿਰ ਉਪਰ ਛਤ੍ਰ ਫਿਰਾਇਆ ॥
verses.transliteration: va'ddaa beer akhai kai sir upar chhatr firaiaa ||
verses.translation: He was called a great hero and had a canopy moving over his head.

verses.gurmukhi: ਦਿੱਤਾ ਇੰਦੁ੍ਰ ਨਿਕਾਲ ਕੈ ਤਿਨ ਗਿਰਿ ਕੈਲਾਸੁ ਤਕਾਇਆ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhi'taa i(n)dhur nikaal kai tin gir kailaas takaiaa ||
verses.translation: Indra was turned out of his kingdom and he looked towards the Kailash mountain.

verses.gurmukhi: ਡਰਿ ਕੈ ਹੱਥੋ ਦਾਨਵੀ ਦਿਲ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਤ੍ਰਾਸੁ ਵਧਾਇਆ ॥
verses.transliteration: ddar kai ha'tho dhaanavee dhil a(n)dhar traas vadhaiaa ||
verses.translation: Frightened by the demons, the element of fear grew enormously in his heart

verses.gurmukhi: ਪਾਸ ਦੁਰਗਾ ਦੇ ਇੰਦੁ੍ਰ ਆਇਆ ॥੩॥
verses.transliteration: paas dhuragaa dhe i(n)dhur aaiaa ||3||
verses.translation: He came, therefore to Durga.3.

verses.gurmukhi: ਇਕ ਦਿਹਾੜੇ ਨਾਵਣ ਆਈ ਦੁਰਗਸਾਹ ॥
verses.transliteration: eik dhihaaRe naavan aaiee dhuragasaeh ||
verses.translation: One day Durga came for a bath.

verses.gurmukhi: ਇੰਦ੍ਰ ਬਿਰਥਾ ਸੁਣਾਈ ਅਪਣੇ ਹਾਲ ਦੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: ei(n)dhr birathaa sunaiee apane haal dhee ||
verses.translation: Indra related to her the story agony:

verses.gurmukhi: ਛੀਨ ਲਈ ਠਕੁਰਾਈ ਸਾਤੇ ਦਾਨਵੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: chheen liee Thakuraiee saate dhaanavee ||
verses.translation: “The demons have seized from us our kingdom."

verses.gurmukhi: ਲੋਕੀ ਤਿਹੀ ਫਿਰਾਈ ਦੋਹੀ ਆਪਣੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: lokee tihee firaiee dhohee aapanee ||
verses.translation: “They have proclaimed their authority over all the three worlds."

verses.gurmukhi: ਬੈਠੇ ਵਾਇ ਵਧਾਈ ਤੇ ਅਮਰਾਵਤੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: baiThe vai vadhaiee te amaraavatee ||
verses.translation: “They have played musical instruments in their rejoicings in Amaravati, the city of gods."

verses.gurmukhi: ਦਿੱਤੇ ਦੇਵ ਭਜਾਈ ਸਭਨਾ ਰਾਕਸਾਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhi'te dhev bhajaiee sabhanaa raakasaa(n) ||
verses.translation: “All the demons have caused the flight of the gods."

verses.gurmukhi: ਕਿਨੈ ਨ ਜਿੱਤਾ ਜਾਈ ਮੱਹਖੇ ਦੈਤ ਨੂੰ ॥
verses.transliteration: kinai na ji'taa jaiee ma'hakhe dhait noo(n) ||
verses.translation: “None hath gone and conquered Mahikha, the demon."

verses.gurmukhi: ਤੇਰੀ ਸਾਮ ਤਕਾਈ ਦੇਵੀ ਦੁਰਗਸਾਹ ॥੪॥
verses.transliteration: teree saam takaiee dhevee dhuragasaeh ||4||
verses.translation: “O goddess Durga, I have come under Thy refuge.”4.

verses.gurmukhi: ਦੁਰਗਾ ਬੈਣ ਸੁਣੰਦੀ ਹੱਸੀ ਹੜਹੜਾਇ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhuragaa bain suna(n)dhee ha'see haRahaRai ||
verses.translation: Listening to these words (of Indra), Durga laughed heartily.

verses.gurmukhi: ਓਹੀ ਸੀਹੁ ਮੰਗਾਇਆ ਰਾਖਸ ਭੱਖਣਾ ॥
verses.transliteration: ohee seeh ma(n)gaiaa raakhas bha'khanaa ||
verses.translation: She sent for that lion, who was she devourer of demons.

verses.gurmukhi: ਚਿੰਤਾ ਕਰਹੁ ਨ ਕਾਈ ਦੇਵਾ ਨੂੰ ਆਖਿਆ ॥
verses.transliteration: chi(n)taa karahu na kaiee dhevaa noo(n) aakhiaa ||
verses.translation: She said to gods, “Do not worry mother any more.”

verses.gurmukhi: ਰੋਹ ਹੋਈ ਮਹਾ ਮਾਈ ਰਾਕਸਿ ਮਾਰਣੇ ॥੫॥
verses.transliteration: roh hoiee mahaa maiee raakas maarane ||5||
verses.translation: For killing the demons, the great mother exhibited great fury.5.

verses.gurmukhi: ਦੋਹਰਾ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhoharaa ||
verses.translation: DOHRA

verses.gurmukhi: ਰਾਕਸਿ ਆਏ ਰੋਹਲੇ ਖੇਤਿ ਭਿੜਨ ਕੇ ਚਾਇ ॥
verses.transliteration: raakas aae rohale khet bhiRan ke chai ||
verses.translation: The infuriated demons came with the desire of fighting in the battlefield.

verses.gurmukhi: ਲਸ਼ਕਨਿ ਤੇਗਾਂ ਬਰਛੀਆਂ ਸੂਰਜੁ ਨਦਰਿ ਨ ਪਾਇ ॥੬॥
verses.transliteration: lashakan tegaa(n) barachheeaa(n) sooraj nadhar na pai ||6||
verses.translation: The swords and daggers glisten with such brilliance that the sun cannot be seen.6.

verses.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: pauRee ||
verses.translation: PAURI

verses.gurmukhi: ਦੁਹਾਂ ਕੰਧਾਰਾ ਮੁਹਿ ਜੁੜੇ ਢੋਲ ਸੰਖ ਨਗਾਰੇ ਬਜੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhuhaa(n) ka(n)dhaaraa muh juRe ddol sa(n)kh nagaare baje ||
verses.translation: Both the armies faced each other and the drums, conches and trumpets sounded.

verses.gurmukhi: ਰਾਕਸਿ ਆਏ ਰੋਹਲੇ ਤਰਵਾਰੀ ਬਖਤਰ ਸੱਜੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: raakas aae rohale taravaaree bakhatar sa'je ||
verses.translation: The demons came in great rage, decorated with swords and armour.

verses.gurmukhi: ਜੱੁਟੇ ਸਉਹੇ ਜੱੁਧ ਨੰੁ ਇਕ ਜਾਤ ਨ ਜਾਣਨ ਭੱਜੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: ja'uTe sauhe ja'udh na(n) ik jaat na jaanan bha'je ||
verses.translation: The warriors were facing the war-front and none of them knows to retrace his steps.

verses.gurmukhi: ਖੇਤ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਜੋਧੇ ਗੱਜੇ ॥੭॥
verses.transliteration: khet a(n)dhar jodhe ga'je ||7||
verses.translation: The brave fighters were roaring in the battlefield.7.

verses.gurmukhi: ਜੰਗ ਮੁਸਾਫਾ ਬੱਜਿਆ ਰਣਿ ਘੁਰੇ ਨਗਾਰੇ ਚਾਵਲੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: ja(n)g musaafaa ba'jiaa ran ghure nagaare chaavale ||
verses.translation: The war-trumpet sounded and the enthusiastic drums thundered in the battlefield.

verses.gurmukhi: ਝੂਲਣ ਨੇਜੇ ਬੈਰਕਾ ਨੀਸਾਣ ਲਸਨਿ ਲਸਾਵਲੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: jhoolan neje bairakaa neesaan lasan lasaavale ||
verses.translation: The lances swung and the lustrous tassels of the banners glistened.

verses.gurmukhi: ਢੋਲ ਨਗਾਰੇ ਪਉਣ ਦੇ ਊਂਘਨ ਜਾਣੁ ਜਟਾਵਲੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: ddol nagaare paun dhe uoo(n)ghan jaan jaTaavale ||
verses.translation: The drums and trumpets echoed and the worriors were dozing like the drunkard with matted hair.

verses.gurmukhi: ਦੁਰਗਾ ਦਾਨੋ ਡਹੇ ਰਣ ਨਾਦ ਵੱਜਨ ਖੇਤੁ ਭੀਹਾਵਲੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhuragaa dhaano ddahe ran naadh va'jan khet bheehaavale ||
verses.translation: Durga and demons waged war in the battlefield where dreadful music is being played.

verses.gurmukhi: ਬੀਰ ਪਰੋਤੇ ਬਰਛੀੲਂੇ ਜਣ ਡਾਲ ਚਮੱੁਟੇ ਆਵਲੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: beer parote barachhe'ee(n)e jan ddaal chama'uTe aavale ||
verses.translation: The brave fighters were pierced by daggers like the phylianthus emblica sticking with the bough.

verses.gurmukhi: ਇਕ ਵੱਢੇ ਤੇਗੀ ਤੜਫੀਅਨ ਮਦ ਪੀਤੇ ਲੋਟਨਿ ਬਾਵਲੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: eik va'dde tegee taRafeean madh peete loTan baavale ||
verses.translation: Some writhe being chopped by the sword like the rolling mad drunkards.

verses.gurmukhi: ਇਕ ਚੁਣ ਚੁਣ ਝਾੜਉ ਕਢੀਅਨ ਰੇਤ ਵਿਚੋ ਸੁਇਨਾ ਡਾਵਲੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: eik chun chun jhaaRau kaddeean ret vicho suinaa ddaavale ||
verses.translation: Some are picked up from the bushes like the process of panning out gold from the sand.

verses.gurmukhi: ਗਦਾ ਤ੍ਰਿਸੂਲਾਂ ਬਰਛੀਆਂ ਤੀਰ ਵੱਗਨ ਖਰੇ ਉਤਾਵਲੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: gadhaa tirasoolaa(n) barachheeaa(n) teer va'gan khare utaavale ||
verses.translation: The maces, tridents, daggers and arrows are being struck with real hurry.

verses.gurmukhi: ਜਣ ਡਸੇ ਭੁਜੰਗਮ ਸਾਵਲੇ॥ ਮਰ ਜਾਵਨਿ ਬੀਰ ਰੁਹਾਵਲੇ ॥੮॥
verses.transliteration: jan ddase bhuja(n)gam saavale|| mar jaavan beer ruhaavale ||8||
verses.translation: It appears that black snakes are stinging and the furious heroes are dying.8.

verses.gurmukhi: ਦੇਖਣ ਚੰਡ ਪ੍ਰਚੰਡ ਨੰੂ ਰਣ ਘੁਰੇ ਨਗਾਰੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhekhan cha(n)dd pracha(n)dd na(n)oo ran ghure nagaare ||
verses.translation: Seeing the intense glory of Chandi, the trumpets souded in the battlefield.

verses.gurmukhi: ਧਾਏ ਰਾਕਸਿ ਰੋਹਲੇ ਚਉਗਿਰਦੇ ਭਾਰੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhaae raakas rohale chaugiradhe bhaare ||
verses.translation: The highly furious demons ran on all four sides.

verses.gurmukhi: ਹਥੀਂ ਤੇਗਾਂ ਪਕੜਿ ਕੈ ਰਣ ਭਿੜੇ ਕਰਾਰੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: hathee(n) tegaa(n) pakaR kai ran bhiRe karaare ||
verses.translation: Holding their swords in their hands they fought very bravely in the battlefield.

verses.gurmukhi: ਕਦੇ ਨ ਨੱਠੈ ਜੱੁਧ ਤੇ ਜੋਧੇ ਜੁਝਾਰੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: kadhe na na'Thai ja'udh te jodhe jujhaare ||
verses.translation: These militant fighters never ran away from war-arena.

verses.gurmukhi: ਦਿਲ ਵਿਚ ਰੋਹ ਬਢਾਇ ਕੈ ਮਾਰਿ ਮਾਰਿ ਪੁਕਾਰੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhil vich roh baddai kai maar maar pukaare ||
verses.translation: Highly infuriated they shouted “kill, kill” in their ranks.

verses.gurmukhi: ਮਾਰੇ ਚੰਡ ਪ੍ਰਚੰਡ ਨੈ ਬੀਰ ਖੇਤ ਉਤਾਰੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: maare cha(n)dd pracha(n)dd nai beer khet utaare ||
verses.translation: The intensely glorious Chandi killed the warriors and threw them in the field.

verses.gurmukhi: ਮਾਰੇ ਜਾਪਨ ਬਿਜੁਲੀ ਸਿਰਭਾਰਿ ਮੁਨਾਰੇ ॥੯॥
verses.transliteration: maare jaapan bijulee sirabhaar munaare ||9||
verses.translation: It appeared that the lightning had eradicated the minarets and thrown them headlong.9.

verses.gurmukhi: ਚੋਟ ਪਈ ਦਮਾਮੇ ਦਲਾਂ ਮੁਕਾਬਲਾ ॥
verses.transliteration: choT piee dhamaame dhalaa(n) mukaabalaa ||
verses.translation: The drum was beaten and the armies attacked each other.

verses.gurmukhi: ਦੇਵੀ ਦਸਤ ਨਚਾਈ ਸੀਹਣ ਸਾਰਦੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhevee dhasat nachaiee seehan saaradhee ||
verses.translation: The goddess caused the dancing of the lioness of steel (sword)

verses.gurmukhi: ਪੇਟਿ ਮਲੰਦੇ ਲਾਈ ਮਹਖੇ ਦੈਤ ਨੰੂ ॥
verses.transliteration: peT mala(n)dhe laiee mahakhe dhait na(n)oo ||
verses.translation: And gave a blow to the demon Mahisha who was rubbing his belly.

verses.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਦੇ ਆਂਦਾ ਖਾਈ ਨਾਲੇ ਰੁਕੜੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: gurdhe aa(n)dhaa khaiee naale rukaRe ||
verses.translation: (The sword) pierced the kindneys, intestines and the ribs.

verses.gurmukhi: ਜੇਹੀ ਦਿਲ ਵਿਚ ਆਈ ਕਹੀ ਸੁਣਾਇ ਕੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: jehee dhil vich aaiee kahee sunai kai ||
verses.translation: Whatever hath come in my mind, I have related that.

verses.gurmukhi: ਚੋਟੀ ਜਾਣ ਦਿਖਾਈ ਤਾਰੇ ਧੂਮਕੇਤ ॥੧੦॥
verses.transliteration: choTee jaan dhikhaiee taare dhoomaket ||10||
verses.translation: It appears that Dhumketu (the shooting star) had displayed its top-knot.10.

verses.gurmukhi: ਚੋਟਾਂ ਪਵਨ ਨਗਾਰੇ ਅਣੀਆਂ ਜੱਟੀਆਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: choTaa(n) pavan nagaare aneeaa(n) ja'Teeaa(n) ||
verses.translation: The drums are being beaten and the armies are engaged in close fight with each other.

verses.gurmukhi: ਧੂਹ ਲਈਆਂ ਤਰਵਾਰੀ ਦੇਵਾਂ ਦਾਨਵੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhooh lieeaa(n) taravaaree dhevaa(n) dhaanavee ||
verses.translation: The gods and demons have drawn their swords.

verses.gurmukhi: ਵਾਹਨ ਵਾਰੋ ਵਾਰੀ ਸੂਰੇ ਸੰਘਰੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: vaahan vaaro vaaree soore sa(n)ghare ||
verses.translation: And strike them again and again killing warriors.

verses.gurmukhi: ਵਗੈ ਰੱਤੁ ਝੁਲਾਰੀ ਜਿਉ ਗੇਰੂ ਬਾਬਤ੍ਰਾ ॥
verses.transliteration: vagai ra't jhulaaree jiau geroo baabatraa ||
verses.translation: The blood flows like waterfall in the same manner as the red ochre colour is washed off from clothes.

verses.gurmukhi: ਦੇਖਨ ਬੈਠ ਅਟਾਰੀ ਨਾਰੀ ਰਾਕਸਾਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhekhan baiTh aTaaree naaree raakasaa(n) ||
verses.translation: The ladies of demons see the fight, while sitting in their lofts.

verses.gurmukhi: ਪਾਈ ਧੂਮ ਸਵਾਰੀ ਦੁਰਗਾ ਦਾਨਵੀ ॥੧੧॥
verses.transliteration: paiee dhoom savaaree dhuragaa dhaanavee ||11||
verses.translation: The carriage of the goddess Durga hath raised a tumult amongst the demons.11.

verses.gurmukhi: ਲੱਖ ਨਗਾਰੇ ਵੱਜਨ ਆਮ੍ਹੋ ਸਾਮ੍ਹਣੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: la'kh nagaare va'jan aamho saamhane ||
verses.translation: A hundred thousand trumpets resound facing one another.

verses.gurmukhi: ਰਾਕਸ ਰਣੋ ਨ ਭੱਜਨ ਰੋਹੇ ਰੋਹਲੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: raakas rano na bha'jan rohe rohale ||
verses.translation: The highly infuriated demons do not flee from the battlefield.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸੀਹਾਂ ਵਾਂਗੂ ਗੱਜਣ ਸੱਭੇ ਸੂਰਮੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: seehaa(n) vaa(n)goo ga'jan sa'bhe soorame ||
verses.translation: All the warriors roar like lions.

verses.gurmukhi: ਤਣਿ ਤਣਿ ਕੈਬਰ ਛੱਡਨ ਦੁਰਗਾ ਸਾਮਣੇ ॥੧੨॥
verses.transliteration: tan tan kaibar chha'ddan dhuragaa saamane ||12||
verses.translation: They stretch their bows and shoot the arrows in front it Durga.12.

verses.gurmukhi: ਘੁਰੇ ਨਗਾਰੇ ਦੋਹਰੇ ਰਣ ਸੰਗਲੀਆਲੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: ghure nagaare dhohare ran sa(n)galeeaale ||
verses.translation: The dual chained trumpets sounded in the battlefield.

verses.gurmukhi: ਧੂੜਿ ਲਪੇਟੇ ਧੂਹਰੇ ਸਿਰਦਾਰ ਜਟਾਲੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhooR lapeTe dhoohare siradhaar jaTaale ||
verses.translation: The demon chieftains having matted locks are enveloped in dust.

verses.gurmukhi: ਉਖਲੀਆਂ ਨਾਸਾ ਜਿਨਾ ਮੁਹਿ ਜਾਪਨ ਆਲੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: aukhaleeaa(n) naasaa jinaa muh jaapan aale ||
verses.translation: Their nostrils are like mortars and the mouths seem like niches.

verses.gurmukhi: ਧਾਏ ਦੇਵੀ ਸਾਹਮਣੇ ਬੀਰ ਮੱੁਛਲੀਆਲੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhaae dhevee saahamane beer ma'uchhaleeaale ||
verses.translation: The brave fighters bearing long moustaches ran in front of the goddess.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸੁਰਪਤ ਜੇਹੇ ਲੜ ਹਟੇ ਬੀਰ ਟਲੇ ਨ ਟਾਲੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: surapat jehe laR haTe beer Tale na Taale ||
verses.translation: The warriors like the king of gods (Indra) had become tired of fighting, but the brave fighters could not be averted from their stand.

verses.gurmukhi: ਗੱਜੇ ਦੁਰਗਾ ਘੇਰਿ ਕੈ ਜਣੁ ਘਣੀਅਰੁ ਕਾਲੇ ॥੧੩॥
verses.transliteration: ga'je dhuragaa gher kai jan ghaneear kaale ||13||
verses.translation: They roared. On besieging Durga, like dark clouds.13.

verses.gurmukhi: ਚੋਟ ਪਈ ਖਰਚਾਮੀ ਦਲਾਂ ਮੁਕਾਬਲਾ ॥
verses.transliteration: choT piee kharachaamee dhalaa(n) mukaabalaa ||
verses.translation: The drum, wrapped in donkey’s hide, was beaten and the armies attacked each other.

verses.gurmukhi: ਘੇਰ ਲਈ ਵਰਿਆਮੀ ਦੁਰਗਾ ਆਇ ਕੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: gher liee variaamee dhuragaa aai kai ||
verses.translation: The brave demon-warriors besieged Durga.

verses.gurmukhi: ਰਾਖਸ ਵਡੇ ਅਲਾਮੀ ਭੱਜ ਨ ਜਾਣਦੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: raakhas vadde alaamee bha'j na jaanadhe ||
verses.translation: They are greatly knowledgeable in warfare and do not know running back.

verses.gurmukhi: ਅੰਤ ਹੋਏ ਸੁਰਗਾਮੀ ਮਾਰੇ ਦੇਵਤਾ ॥੧੪॥
verses.transliteration: a(n)t hoe suragaamee maare dhevataa ||14||
verses.translation: They ultimately went to heaven on being killed by the goddess.14.

verses.gurmukhi: ਅਗਣਤ ਘੁਰੇ ਨਗਾਰੇ ਦਲਾਂ ਭਿੜੰਦਿਆਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: aganat ghure nagaare dhalaa(n) bhiRa(n)dhiaa(n) ||
verses.translation: With the flaring up of fight between the armies, innumerable trumpets sounded.

verses.gurmukhi: ਪਾਏ ਮਹਖਲ ਭਾਰੇ ਦੇਵਾ ਦਾਨਵਾਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: paae mahakhal bhaare dhevaa dhaanavaa(n) ||
verses.translation: The gods and demons both have raised great tumult like male buffalos.

verses.gurmukhi: ਵਾਹਨ ਫੱਟ ਕਰਾਰੇ ਰਾਕਸਿ ਰੋਹਲੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: vaahan fa'T karaare raakas rohale ||
verses.translation: The infuriated demons strike strong blows causing wounds.

verses.gurmukhi: ਜਾਪਣ ਤੇਗੀ ਆਰੇ ਮਿਆਨੋ ਧੂਹੀਆਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: jaapan tegee aare miaano dhooheeaa(n) ||
verses.translation: It appears that the sword pulled from the scabbards are like saws.

verses.gurmukhi: ਜੋਧੇ ਵਡੇ ਮੁਨਾਰੇ ਜਾਪਨ ਖੇਤ ਵਿਚਿ ॥
verses.transliteration: jodhe vadde munaare jaapan khet vich ||
verses.translation: The warriors look like high minarets in the battlefield.

verses.gurmukhi: ਦੇਵੀ ਆਪ ਸਵਾਰੇ ਪਬ ਜਵੇਹਣੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhevee aap savaare pab javehane ||
verses.translation: The goddess herself killed these mountain-like demons.

verses.gurmukhi: ਕਦੇ ਨ ਆਖਨ ਹਾਰੇ ਧਾਵਨ ਸਾਹਮਣੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: kadhe na aakhan haare dhaavan saahamane ||
verses.translation: They never uttered the word ‘defeat’ and ran in front of the goddess.

verses.gurmukhi: ਦੁਰਗਾ ਸਭ ਸੰਘਾਰੇ ਰਾਖਸਿ ਖੜਗ ਲੈ ॥੧੫॥
verses.transliteration: dhuragaa sabh sa(n)ghaare raakhas khaRag lai ||15||
verses.translation: Durga, holding her sword, killed all the demons.15.

verses.gurmukhi: ਉਮਲ ਲੱਥੇ ਜੋਧੇ ਮਾਰੂ ਬੱਜਿਆ ॥
verses.transliteration: aumal la'the jodhe maaroo ba'jiaa ||
verses.translation: The fatal martial music sounded and the warriors came in the battlefield with enthusiasm.

verses.gurmukhi: ਬੱਦਲ ਜਿਉ ਮਹਿਖਾਸੁਰ ਰਣ ਵਿਚਿ ਗੱਜਿਆ ॥
verses.transliteration: ba'dhal jiau mahikhaasur ran vich ga'jiaa ||
verses.translation: Mahishasura thundered in the field like the cloud

verses.gurmukhi: ਇੰਦ੍ਰ ਜੇਹਾ ਜੋਧਾ ਮੈਥਉ ਭੱਜਿਆ ॥
verses.transliteration: ei(n)dhr jehaa jodhaa maithau bha'jiaa ||
verses.translation: “The warrior like Indra fled from me

verses.gurmukhi: ਕਉਣ ਵਿਚਾਰੀ ਦੁਰਗਾ ਜਿਨ ਰਣੁ ਸਜਿਆ ॥੧੬॥
verses.transliteration: kaun vichaaree dhuragaa jin ran sajiaa ||16||
verses.translation: “Who is this wretched Durga, who hath come to srart war with me?”16.

verses.gurmukhi: ਵੱਜੇ ਢੋਲ ਨਗਾਰੇ ਦਲਾਂ ਮੁਕਾਬਲਾ ॥
verses.transliteration: va'je ddol nagaare dhalaa(n) mukaabalaa ||
verses.translation: The drums and trumpets have sounded and the armies have attacked each other.

verses.gurmukhi: ਤੀਰ ਫਿਰੈ ਰੈਬਾਰੇ ਆਮ੍ਹੋ ਸਾਮ੍ਹਣੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: teer firai raibaare aamho saamhane ||
verses.translation: The arrows move opposite to each other guidingly.

verses.gurmukhi: ਅਗਣਤ ਬੀਰ ਸੰਘਾਰੇ ਲਗਦੀ ਕੈਬਰੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: aganat beer sa(n)ghaare lagadhee kaibaree ||
verses.translation: With the infliction of arrows countless warriors have been killed.

verses.gurmukhi: ਡਿੱਗੇ ਜਾਣਿ ਮੁਨਾਰੇ ਮਾਰੈ ਬਿੱਜੁ ਦੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: ddi'ge jaan munaare maarai bi'j dhe ||
verses.translation: Falling like the minarets smote by lightning.

verses.gurmukhi: ਖੁੱਲੀ ਵਾਲੀ ਦੈਤ ਅਹਾੜੇ ਸਭੇ ਸੂਰਮੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: khu'lee vaalee dhait ahaaRe sabhe soorame ||
verses.translation: All the demon-fighters with untied hair shouted in agony.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸੱੁਤੇ ਜਾਣਿ ਜਟਾਰੇ ਭੰਗਾ ਖਾਇ ਕੈ ॥੧੭॥
verses.transliteration: sa'ute jaan jaTaare bha(n)gaa khai kai ||17||
verses.translation: It seems that the hermits with matted locks are sleeping after eating the intoxicating hemps.17.

verses.gurmukhi: ਦੁਹਾਂ ਕੰਧਾਰਾਂ ਮੁਹਿ ਜੁੜੇ ਨਾਲਿ ਧਉਸਾ ਭਾਰੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhuhaa(n) ka(n)dhaaraa(n) muh juRe naal dhausaa bhaaree ||
verses.translation: Both the armies are facing each other alongwith the resounding big trumpet.

verses.gurmukhi: ਕੜਕ ਉੱਠਿਆ ਫਉਜ ਤੇ ਵਡਾ ਅਹੰਕਾਰੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: kaRak u'Thiaa fauj te vaddaa aha(n)kaaree ||
verses.translation: The highly egoist warrior of the army thundered.

verses.gurmukhi: ਲੈ ਕੈ ਚਲਿਆ ਸੂਰਮੇ ਨਾਲਿ ਵਡੇ ਹਜਾਰੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: lai kai chaliaa soorame naal vadde hajaaree ||
verses.translation: He is moving towards the war-arena with thousands of mighty warriors.

verses.gurmukhi: ਮਿਆਨੋ ਖੰਡਾ ਧੂਹਿਆ ਮਹਿਖਾਸੁਰ ਭਾਰੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: miaano kha(n)ddaa dhoohiaa mahikhaasur bhaaree ||
verses.translation: Mahishasura pulled out his huge double-edged sword from his scabbard.

verses.gurmukhi: ਉੱਮਲ ਲੱਥੇ ਸੂਰਮੇ ਮਾਰ ਮਚੀ ਕਰਾਰੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: au'mal la'the soorame maar machee karaaree ||
verses.translation: The fighters entered the field enthusiastically and there occurred formidable fighting.

verses.gurmukhi: ਜਾਪੇ ਚੱਲੇ ਰੱਤ ਦੇ ਸਲਲੇ ਜਟਧਾਰੀ ॥੧੮॥
verses.transliteration: jaape cha'le ra't dhe salale jaTadhaaree ||18||
verses.translation: It appears that the blood flows like the water (of Ganges) from the tangled hair of Shiva.18.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸੱਟ ਪਈ ਜਮਧਾਣੀ ਦਲਾਂ ਮੁਕਾਬਲਾ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa'T piee jamadhaanee dhalaa(n) mukaabalaa ||
verses.translation: When the trumpet, enveloped by the hide of the male buffalo, the vehicle of Yama, sounded, the armies attacked each other.

verses.gurmukhi: ਧੂਹਿ ਲਈ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾਣੀ ਦੁਰਗਾ ਮਿਆਨ ਤੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhooh liee kirapaanee dhuragaa miaan te ||
verses.translation: Durga pulled her sword from the scabbard.

verses.gurmukhi: ਚੰਡੀ ਰਾਕਸਿ ਖਾਣੀ ਵਾਹੀ ਦੈਤ ਨੂੰ ॥
verses.transliteration: cha(n)ddee raakas khaanee vaahee dhait noo(n) ||
verses.translation: She struck the demon with that Chandi, the devourer of demons (that is the sword).

verses.gurmukhi: ਕੋਪਰ ਚੂਰ ਚਵਾਣੀ ਲੱਥੀ ਕਰਗ ਲੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: kopar choor chavaanee la'thee karag lai ||
verses.translation: It broke the skull and face into pieces and pierced through the skeleton.

verses.gurmukhi: ਪਾਖਰ ਤੁਰਾ ਪਲਾਣੀ ਰੜਕੀ ਧਰਤ ਜਾਇ ॥
verses.transliteration: paakhar turaa palaanee raRakee dharat jai ||
verses.translation: And it further pierced through the saddle and caparison of the horse, and struck on the earth supported by the Bull (Dhaul).

verses.gurmukhi: ਲੈਦੀ ਅਘਾ ਸਿਧਾਣੀ ਸਿੰਗਾਂ ਧਉਲ ਦਿਆਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: laidhee aghaa sidhaanee si(n)gaa(n) dhaul dhiaa(n) ||
verses.translation: It moved further and struck the horns of the Bull.

verses.gurmukhi: ਕੂਰਮ ਸਿਰ ਲਹਿਲਾਣੀ ਦੁਸਮਨ ਮਾਰਿ ਕੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: kooram sir lahilaanee dhusaman maar kai ||
verses.translation: Then it struck on the Tortoise supporting the Bull and thus killing the enemy.

verses.gurmukhi: ਵੱਢੇ ਗਨ ਤਿਖਾਣੀ ਮੂਏ ਖੇਤ ਵਿਚ ॥
verses.transliteration: va'dde gan tikhaanee mooe khet vich ||
verses.translation: The demons are lying dead in the battlefield like the pieces of wood sawed by the carpenter.

verses.gurmukhi: ਰਣ ਵਿਚ ਘੱਤੀ ਘਾਣੀ ਲੋਹੂ ਮਿਝ ਦੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: ran vich gha'tee ghaanee lohoo mijh dhee ||
verses.translation: The press of blood and marrow has been set in motion in the battlefield.

verses.gurmukhi: ਚਾਰੇ ਜੁਗ ਕਹਾਣੀ ਚੱਲਗ ਤੇਗ ਦੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: chaare jug kahaanee cha'lag teg dhee ||
verses.translation: The story of the sword will be related in all the four ages.

verses.gurmukhi: ਬਿਧਣ ਖੇਤ ਵਿਹਾਣੀ ਮਹਖੇ ਦੈਤ ਨੂੰ ॥੧੯॥
verses.transliteration: bidhan khet vihaanee mahakhe dhait noo(n) ||19||
verses.translation: On the demon Mahisha the period of agony occurred in the battlefield.19.

verses.gurmukhi: ਇਤੀ ਮਹਖਾਸੁਰ ਦੈਤ ਮਾਰੇ ਦੁਰਗਾ ਆਇਆ ॥
verses.transliteration: eitee mahakhaasur dhait maare dhuragaa aaiaa ||
verses.translation: In this way the demon Mahishasura was killed on the arrival of Durga.

verses.gurmukhi: ਚਉਦਹ ਲੋਕਾਂ ਰਾਣੀ ਸਿੰਘੁ ਨਚਾਇਆ ॥
verses.transliteration: chaudheh lokaa(n) raanee si(n)gh nachaiaa ||
verses.translation: The queen caused the lion to dance in the fourteen worlds.

verses.gurmukhi: ਮਾਰੇ ਬੀਰ ਜਟਾਣੀ ਦਲ ਵਿਚਿ ਅਗਲੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: maare beer jaTaanee dhal vich agale ||
verses.translation: She killed a great number of brave demons with matted locks in the battlefield.

verses.gurmukhi: ਮੰਗਨ ਨਾਹੀ ਪਾਣੀ ਦਲੀ ਹੰਕਾਰ ਕੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: ma(n)gan naahee paanee dhalee ha(n)kaar kai ||
verses.translation: Challenging the armies, these warriors do not even ask for water.

verses.gurmukhi: ਜਣ ਕਰੀ ਸਮਾਇ ਪਠਾਣੀ ਸੁਣਿ ਕੈ ਰਾਗੁ ਨੂੰ ॥
verses.transliteration: jan karee samai paThaanee sun kai raag noo(n) ||
verses.translation: It seems that listening to the music, the Pathans have realized the state of ecstasy.

verses.gurmukhi: ਰੱਤੂ ਦੇ ਹੜਵਾਣੀ ਚਲੇ ਬੀਰ ਖੇਤ ॥
verses.transliteration: ra'too dhe haRavaanee chale beer khet ||
verses.translation: The flood of the blood of the fighters is flowing.

verses.gurmukhi: ਪੀਤਾ ਫੁੱਲ ਇਆਣੀ ਘੁਮਨ ਸੂਰਮੇ ॥੨੦॥
verses.transliteration: peetaa fu'l iaanee ghuman soorame ||20||
verses.translation: The brave warriors are roaming as if they have ignorantly consumed the intoxicating poppy.20.

verses.gurmukhi: ਹੋਈ ਅਲੋਪ ਭਵਾਨੀ ਦੇਵਾਂ ਨੂੰ ਰਾਜੁ ਦੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: hoiee alop bhavaanee dhevaa(n) noo(n) raaj dhe ||
verses.translation: Bhavani (Durga) disappeard after bestowing kingdom on the gods.

verses.gurmukhi: ਈਸਰ ਦੀ ਬਰਦਾਨੀ ਹੋਈ ਜਿੱਤੁ ਦਿਨ ॥
verses.transliteration: e'eesar dhee baradhaanee hoiee ji't dhin ||
verses.translation: The day for which Shiva granted the boon.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸੁੰਭ ਨਿਸੁੰਭ ਗੁਮਾਨੀ ਜਨਮੇ ਸੂਰਮੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: su(n)bh nisu(n)bh gumaanee janame soorame ||
verses.translation: The proud warriors Sumbh and Nisumbh were born.

verses.gurmukhi: ਇੰਦ੍ਰ ਦੀ ਰਜਧਾਨੀ ਤੱਕੀ ਜਿੱਤਣੀ ॥੨੧॥
verses.transliteration: ei(n)dhr dhee rajadhaanee ta'kee ji'tanee ||21||
verses.translation: They planned to conquer the capital of Indra.21.

verses.gurmukhi: ਇੰਦ੍ਰਪੁਰੀ ਤੇ ਧਾਵਣਾ ਵਡ ਜੋਧੀ ਮਤਾ ਪਕਾਇਆ ॥
verses.transliteration: ei(n)dhrapuree te dhaavanaa vadd jodhee mataa pakaiaa ||
verses.translation: The great fighters decided to rush towards the kingdom of Indra.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸੰਜ ਪਟੇਲਾ ਪਾਖਰਾ ਭੇੜ ਸੰਦਾ ਸਾਜੁ ਬਣਾਇਆ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa(n)j paTelaa paakharaa bheR sa(n)dhaa saaj banaiaa ||
verses.translation: They began to prepare the war-material consisting of armour with belts and saddle-gear.

verses.gurmukhi: ਜੰਮੇ ਕਟਕ ਅਛੂਹਣੀ ਅਸਮਾਨੁ ਗਰਦੀ ਛਾਇਆ ॥
verses.transliteration: ja(n)me kaTak achhoohanee asamaan garadhee chhaiaa ||
verses.translation: An army of lakhs of warriors gathered and the dust rose to sky.

verses.gurmukhi: ਰੋਹ ਸੁੰਭ ਨਿਸੁੰਭ ਸਿਧਾਇਆ ॥੨੨॥
verses.transliteration: roh su(n)bh nisu(n)bh sidhaiaa ||22||
verses.translation: Sumbh and Nisumbh, full of rage, have marched forward.22.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸੁੰਭ ਨਿਸੁੰਭ ਅਲਾਇਆ ਵਡ ਜੋਧੀ ਸੰਘਰੁ ਵਾਏ ॥
verses.transliteration: su(n)bh nisu(n)bh alaiaa vadd jodhee sa(n)ghar vaae ||
verses.translation: Sumbh and Nisumbh ordered the great warriors to sound the bugle of war.

verses.gurmukhi: ਰੋਹ ਦਿਖਾਲੀ ਦਿੱਤੀਆ ਵਰਿਆਮੀ ਤੁਰੇ ਨਚਾਏ ॥
verses.transliteration: roh dhikhaalee dhi'teeaa variaamee ture nachaae ||
verses.translation: Great fury was visulised and the brave fighters caused the horses to dance.

verses.gurmukhi: ਘੁਰੇ ਦਮਾਮੇ ਦੋਹਰੇ ਜਮ ਬਾਹਣ ਜਿਉ ਅਰੜਾਏ ॥
verses.transliteration: ghure dhamaame dhohare jam baahan jiau araRaae ||
verses.translation: The double-trumpets sounded like the loud voice of the male buffalo, the vehicle of Yama.

verses.gurmukhi: ਦੇਉ ਦਾਨੋ ਲੁੱਝਣ ਆਏ ॥੨੩॥
verses.transliteration: dheau dhaano lu'jhan aae ||23||
verses.translation: The gods and demons have gathered to fight.23.

verses.gurmukhi: ਦਾਨੋ ਦੇਉ ਅਨਾਗੀ ਸੰਘਰੁ ਰਚਿਆ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhaano dheau anaagee sa(n)ghar rachiaa ||
verses.translation: The demons and gods have started a continuous war.

verses.gurmukhi: ਫੱੁਲ ਖਿੜੇ ਜਣ ਬਾਗੀਂ ਬਾਣੇ ਜੋਧਿਆਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: fa'ul khiRe jan baagee(n) baane jodhiaa(n) ||
verses.translation: The garments of the warriors appear like flowers in the garden.

verses.gurmukhi: ਭੂਤਾਂ ਇੱਲਾਂ ਕਾਗੀਂ ਗੋਸਤ ਭਖਿਆ ॥
verses.transliteration: bhootaa(n) i'laa(n) kaagee(n) gosat bhakhiaa ||
verses.translation: The ghosts, vultures and crows have eaten the flesh.

verses.gurmukhi: ਹੁੰਮੜ ਧੁੰਮੜ ਜਾਗੀ ਘੱਤੀ ਸੂਰਿਆਂ ॥੨੪॥
verses.transliteration: hu(n)maR dhu(n)maR jaagee gha'tee sooriaa(n) ||24||
verses.translation: The brave fighters have begun to run about.24.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸੱਟ ਪਈ ਨਗਾਰੇ ਦਲਾਂ ਮੁਕਾਬਲਾ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa'T piee nagaare dhalaa(n) mukaabalaa ||
verses.translation: The trumpet was beaten and the armies attack each other.

verses.gurmukhi: ਦਿਤੇ ਦੇਉ ਭਜਾਈ ਮਿਲਿ ਕੈ ਰਾਕਸੀਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhite dheau bhajaiee mil kai raakasee(n) ||
verses.translation: The demons have gathered together and have caused the gods to flee.

verses.gurmukhi: ਲੋਕੀ ਤਿਹੀ ਫਿਰਾਈ ਦੋਹੀ ਆਪਣੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: lokee tihee firaiee dhohee aapanee ||
verses.translation: They exhibited their authority in the three worlds.

verses.gurmukhi: ਦੁਰਗਾ ਦੀ ਸਾਮ ਤਕਾਈ ਦੇਵਾਂ ਡਰਦਿਆਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhuragaa dhee saam takaiee dhevaa(n) ddaradhiaa(n) ||
verses.translation: The gods, having been frightened went under the refuge of Durga.

verses.gurmukhi: ਆਂਦੀ ਚੰਡਿ ਚੜਾਈ ਉਤੇ ਰਾਕਸਾਂ ॥੨੫॥
verses.transliteration: aa(n)dhee cha(n)dd chaRaiee ute raakasaa(n) ||25||
verses.translation: They caused the goddess Chandi to wage war with demons.25.

verses.gurmukhi: ਆਈ ਫੇਰ ਭਵਾਨੀ ਖਬਰੀ ਪਾਈਆਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: aaiee fer bhavaanee khabaree paieeaa(n) ||
verses.translation: The demons hear the news that the goddess Bhavani has come again.

verses.gurmukhi: ਦੈਤ ਵਡੇ ਅਭਿਮਾਨੀ ਹੋਏ ਏਕਠੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhait vadde abhimaanee hoe ekaThe ||
verses.translation: The highly egoist demons gathered together.

verses.gurmukhi: ਲੋਚਨ ਧੂਮ ਗੁਮਾਨੀ ਰਾਇ ਬੁਲਾਇਆ ॥
verses.transliteration: lochan dhoom gumaanee rai bulaiaa ||
verses.translation: The king Sumbh sent for the egoist Lochan Dhum.

verses.gurmukhi: ਜਗ ਵਿਚ ਵਡਾ ਦਾਨੋ ਆਪ ਕਹਾਇਆ ॥
verses.transliteration: jag vich vaddaa dhaano aap kahaiaa ||
verses.translation: He caused himself to be called the great demon.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸੱਟ ਪਈ ਖਰਚਾਮੀ ਦੁਰਗਾ ਲਿਆਵਣੀ ॥੨੬॥
verses.transliteration: sa'T piee kharachaamee dhuragaa liaavanee ||26||
verses.translation: The drum enveloped with the hide of donkey was struck and it was proclaimed that Durga would be brought.26.

verses.gurmukhi: ਕੜਕ ਉਠੀ ਰਣ ਚੰਡੀ ਫਉਜਾਂ ਦੇਖ ਕੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: kaRak uThee ran cha(n)ddee faujaa(n) dhekh kai ||
verses.translation: Seeing the armies in the battlefield, Chandi shouted loudly.

verses.gurmukhi: ਧੂਹਿ ਮਿਆਨੋ ਖੰਡਾ ਹੋਈ ਸਾਹਮਣੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhooh miaano kha(n)ddaa hoiee saahamane ||
verses.translation: She pulled her double-edged sword from her scabbard and came before the enemy.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸਭੇ ਬੀਰ ਸੰਘਾਰੇ ਧੂਮਰਨੈਣ ਦੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: sabhe beer sa(n)ghaare dhoomaranain dhe ||
verses.translation: She killed all the warriors of Dhumar Nain.

verses.gurmukhi: ਜਣ ਲੈ ਕਟੇ ਆਰੇ ਦਰਖਤ ਬਾਢੀਆਂ ॥੨੭॥
verses.transliteration: jan lai kaTe aare dharakhat baaddeeaa(n) ||27||
verses.translation: It seems that the carpenters have chopped the trees with the saw.27.

verses.gurmukhi: ਚੋਬੀਂ ਧਉਂਸ ਬਜਾਈ ਦਲਾਂ ਮੁਕਾਬਲਾ ॥
verses.transliteration: chobee(n) dhau(n)s bajaiee dhalaa(n) mukaabalaa ||
verses.translation: The drummers sounded the drums and the armies attacked each other.

verses.gurmukhi: ਰੋਹ ਭਵਾਨੀ ਆਈ ਉਤੇ ਰਾਕਸਾਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: roh bhavaanee aaiee ute raakasaa(n) ||
verses.translation: The infuriated Bhavani lodged the attack over the demons.

verses.gurmukhi: ਖੱਬੈ ਦਸਤ ਨਚਾਈ ਸੀਹਣ ਸਾਰ ਦੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: kha'bai dhasat nachaiee seehan saar dhee ||
verses.translation: With her left hand, she caused the dance of the lionss of steel (sword).

verses.gurmukhi: ਬਹੁਤਿਆਂ ਦੇ ਤਨ ਲਾਈ ਕੀਤੀ ਰੰਗੁਲੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: bahutiaa(n) dhe tan laiee keetee ra(n)gulee ||
verses.translation: She struck it on the bodies of many worriors and made it colourful.

verses.gurmukhi: ਭਾਈਆਂ ਮਾਰਨ ਭਾਈ ਦੁਰਗਾ ਜਾਣਿ ਕੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: bhaieeaa(n) maaran bhaiee dhuragaa jaan kai ||
verses.translation: The brothers kill brothers mistaking them for Durga.

verses.gurmukhi: ਰੋਹ ਹੋਇ ਚਲਾਈ ਰਾਕਸਿ ਰਾਇ ਨੰੂ ॥
verses.transliteration: roh hoi chalaiee raakas rai na(n)oo ||
verses.translation: Having been infuriated, she struck it on the king of the demons.

verses.gurmukhi: ਜਮ ਪੁਰ ਦੀਆ ਪਠਾਈ ਲੋਚਨ ਧੂਮ ਨੰੂ ॥
verses.transliteration: jam pur dheeaa paThaiee lochan dhoom na(n)oo ||
verses.translation: Lochan Dhum was sent to the city of Yama.

verses.gurmukhi: ਜਾਪੇ ਦਿੱਤੀ ਸਾਈ ਮਾਰਣ ਸੁੰਭ ਦੀ ॥੨੮॥
verses.transliteration: jaape dhi'tee saiee maaran su(n)bh dhee ||28||
verses.translation: It seems the she gave the advance money for the killing of Sumbh.28.

verses.gurmukhi: ਭੰਨੇ ਦੈਤ ਪੁਕਾਰੇ ਰਾਜੇ ਸੁੰਭ ਥੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: bha(n)ne dhait pukaare raaje su(n)bh thai ||
verses.translation: The demons ran to their king Sumbh and beseeched

verses.gurmukhi: ਲੋਚਨਧੂਮ ਸੰਘਾਰੇ ਸਣੇ ਸਿਪਾਹੀਆਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: lochanadhoom sa(n)ghaare sane sipaaheeaa(n) ||
verses.translation: “Lochan Dhum has been killed alongwith his soldiers

verses.gurmukhi: ਚੁਣਿ ਚੁਣਿ ਜੋਧੇ ਮਾਰੇ ਅੰਦਰ ਖੇਤ ਦੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: chun chun jodhe maare a(n)dhar khet dhai ||
verses.translation: “She hath seleted the warriors and killed them in the battlefield

verses.gurmukhi: ਜਾਪਨ ਅੰਬਰਿ ਤਾਰੇ ਡਿੱਗਨਿ ਸੂਰਮੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: jaapan a(n)bar taare ddi'gan soorame ||
verses.translation: “It seems that the warriors have fallen like the stars from the sky

verses.gurmukhi: ਗਿਰੇ ਪਰਬਤ ਭਾਰੇ ਮਾਰੇ ਬਿੱਜੁ ਦੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: gire parabat bhaare maare bi'j dhai ||
verses.translation: “The huge mountains have fallen, having been smote by the lightning

verses.gurmukhi: ਦੈਤਾਂ ਦੇ ਦਲ ਹਾਰੇ ਦਹਸਤ ਖਾਇ ਕੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhaitaa(n) dhe dhal haare dhahasat khai kai ||
verses.translation: “The forces of the demons have been defeated on becoming panicky

verses.gurmukhi: ਬਚੇ ਸੁ ਮਾਰੇ ਮਾਰੇ ਰਹਦੇ ਰਾਇ ਥੈ ॥੨੯॥
verses.transliteration: bache su maare maare rahadhe rai thai ||29||
verses.translation: “Those who were left have also been killed and the remaining have come to the king.”29.

verses.gurmukhi: ਰੋਹ ਹੋਇ ਬੁਲਾਏ ਰਾਕਸਿ ਰਾਇ ਨੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: roh hoi bulaae raakas rai ne ||
verses.translation: Highly enraged, the king called the demons.

verses.gurmukhi: ਬੈਠੇ ਮਤਾ ਪਕਾਏ ਦੁਰਗਾ ਲਿਆਵਣੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: baiThe mataa pakaae dhuragaa liaavanee ||
verses.translation: They decided to capture Durga.

verses.gurmukhi: ਚੰਡ ਅਰ ਮੁੰਡ ਪਠਾਏ ਬਹੁਤਾ ਕਟਕੁ ਦੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: cha(n)dd ar mu(n)dd paThaae bahutaa kaTak dhai ||
verses.translation: Chand and Mund were sent with huge forces.

verses.gurmukhi: ਜਾਪੇ ਛੱਪਰ ਛਾਏ ਬਣੀਆ ਕੇਜਮਾ ॥
verses.transliteration: jaape chha'par chhaae baneeaa kejamaa ||
verses.translation: It seemed that the swords coming together were like the thatched roofs.

verses.gurmukhi: ਜੇਤੇ ਰਾਇ ਬੁਲਾਇ ਚੱਲੇ ਜੁੱਧ ਨੋ ॥
verses.transliteration: jete rai bulai cha'le ju'dh no ||
verses.translation: All those who were called, marched for war.

verses.gurmukhi: ਜਣ ਜਮ ਪੁਰ ਪਕੜ ਚਲਾਏ ਸਭੇ ਮਾਰਨੇ ॥੩੦॥
verses.transliteration: jan jam pur pakaR chalaae sabhe maarane ||30||
verses.translation: It appear that they were all caught and sent to the city of Yama for killing.30.

verses.gurmukhi: ਢੋਲ ਨਗਾਰੇ ਵਾਏ ਦਲਾਂ ਮੁਕਾਬਲਾ ॥
verses.transliteration: ddol nagaare vaae dhalaa(n) mukaabalaa ||
verses.translation: The drums and trumpets were sounded and the armies attacked each other.

verses.gurmukhi: ਰੋਹ ਰੁਹੇਲੇ ਆਏ ਉਤੇ ਰਾਕਸਾਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: roh ruhele aae ute raakasaa(n) ||
verses.translation: The enraged warriors marched against the demons.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸਭਨੀ ਤੁਰੇ ਨਚਾਏ ਬਰਛੇ ਪਕੜਿ ਕੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: sabhanee ture nachaae barachhe pakaR kai ||
verses.translation: All of them holding their daggers, caused their horses to dance.

verses.gurmukhi: ਬਹੁਤੇ ਮਾਰ ਗਿਰਾਏ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਖੇਤ ਦੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: bahute maar giraae a(n)dhar khet dhai ||
verses.translation: Many were killed and thrown in the battlefield.

verses.gurmukhi: ਤੀਰੀ ਛਹਬਰ ਲਾਈ ਬੱੁਠੀ ਦੇਵਤਾ ॥੩੧॥
verses.transliteration: teeree chhahabar laiee ba'uThee dhevataa ||31||
verses.translation: The arrows shot by the goddess came in showers.31.

verses.gurmukhi: ਭੇਰੀ ਸੰਖ ਵਜਾਏ ਸੰਘਰਿ ਰੱਚਿਆ ॥
verses.transliteration: bheree sa(n)kh vajaae sa(n)ghar ra'chiaa ||
verses.translation: The drums and conches were sounded and the war began.

verses.gurmukhi: ਤਣਿ ਤਣਿ ਤੀਰ ਚਲਾਏ ਦੁਰਗਾ ਧਨਖ ਲੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: tan tan teer chalaae dhuragaa dhanakh lai ||
verses.translation: Durga, taking her bow, stretched it again and again for shooting arrows.

verses.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨੀ ਦਸਤ ਉਠਾਏ ਰਹੇ ਨ ਜੀਵਦੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: jinee dhasat uThaae rahe na jeevadhe ||
verses.translation: Those who raised their hands against the goddess, did not survive.

verses.gurmukhi: ਚੰਡ ਅਰ ਮੁੰਡ ਖਪਾਏ ਦੋਨੋ ਦੇਵਤਾ ॥੩੨॥
verses.transliteration: cha(n)dd ar mu(n)dd khapaae dhono dhevataa ||32||
verses.translation: She destroyed both Chand and Mund.32.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸੁੰਭ ਨਿਸੁੰਭ ਰਿਸਾਏ ਮਾਰੇ ਦੈਤ ਸੁਣ ॥
verses.transliteration: su(n)bh nisu(n)bh risaae maare dhait sun ||
verses.translation: Sumbh and Nisumbh were highly enraged on hearing this killing.

verses.gurmukhi: ਜੋਧੇ ਸਭ ਬੁਲਾਏ ਆਪਣੇ ਮਜਲਸੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: jodhe sabh bulaae aapane majalasee ||
verses.translation: They called all the brave fighters, who were their advisers.

verses.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨੀ ਦੇਉ ਭਜਾਏ ਇੰਦ੍ਰ ਜੇਵਹੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: jinee dheau bhajaae i(n)dhr jevahe ||
verses.translation: Those who had caused the gods like Indra run away.

verses.gurmukhi: ਤੇਈ ਮਾਰ ਗਿਰਾਏ ਪਲ ਵਿਚ ਦੇਵਤਾ ॥
verses.transliteration: teiee maar giraae pal vich dhevataa ||
verses.translation: The goddess killed them in an instant.

verses.gurmukhi: ਓਨੀ ਦਸਤੀ ਦਸਤ ਵਜਾਏ ਤਿਨਾ ਚਿਤ ਕਰਿ ॥
verses.transliteration: onee dhasatee dhasat vajaae tinaa chit kar ||
verses.translation: Keeping Chand Mund in their mind, they rubbed their hands in sorrow.

verses.gurmukhi: ਫਿਰ ਸ੍ਰਣਵਤ ਬੀਜ ਚਲਾਏ ਬੀੜੇ ਰਾਇ ਦੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: fir sranavat beej chalaae beeRe rai dhe ||
verses.translation: Then Sranwat Beej was prepared and sent by the king.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸੰਜ ਪਟੋਲਾ ਪਾਏ ਚਿਲਕਤ ਟੋਪੀਆਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa(n)j paTolaa paae chilakat Topeeaa(n) ||
verses.translation: He wore the armour with belts and the helmet which glistened.

verses.gurmukhi: ਲੁਝੱਣ ਨੋ ਅਰੜਾਏ ਰਾਕਸ ਰੋਹਲੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: lujha'n no araRaae raakas rohale ||
verses.translation: The infuriated demons shouted loudly for war.

verses.gurmukhi: ਕਦੇ ਨ ਕਿਨੇ ਹਟਾਏ ਜੱੁਧ ਮਚਾਇ ਕੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: kadhe na kine haTaae ja'udh machai kai ||
verses.translation: After waging war, none could get their retreat.

verses.gurmukhi: ਮਿਲ ਤੇਈ ਦਾਨੋ ਆਏ ਹੁਣ ਸੰਘਰਿ ਦੇਖਣਾ ॥੩੩॥
verses.transliteration: mil teiee dhaano aae hun sa(n)ghar dhekhanaa ||33||
verses.translation: Such demons have gathered together and come, now see the ensuing war.33.

verses.gurmukhi: ਦੈਤੀ ਡੰਡ ਉਭਾਰੀ ਨੇੜੈ ਆਇ ਕੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhaitee dda(n)dd ubhaaree neRai aai kai ||
verses.translation: On coming near, the demons raised the din.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸਿੰਘ ਕਰੀ ਅਸਵਾਰੀ ਦੁਰਗਾ ਸੋਰ ਸੁਣ ॥
verses.transliteration: si(n)gh karee asavaaree dhuragaa sor sun ||
verses.translation: Hearing this clamour, Durga mounted her lion.

verses.gurmukhi: ਖੱਬੇ ਦਸਤ ਉਭਾਰੀ ਗਦਾ ਫਿਰਾਇ ਕੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: kha'be dhasat ubhaaree gadhaa firai kai ||
verses.translation: She twirled her mace, raising it with her left hand.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸੈਨਾ ਸਭ ਸੰਘਾਰੀ ਸ੍ਰਣਵਤ ਬੀਜ ਦੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: sainaa sabh sa(n)ghaaree sranavat beej dhee ||
verses.translation: She killed all the army of Sranwat Beej.

verses.gurmukhi: ਜਣ ਮਦ ਖਾਇ ਮਦਾਰੀ ਘੂਮਨ ਸੂਰਮੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: jan madh khai madhaaree ghooman soorame ||
verses.translation: It appears that the warriors were roaming like the drug addicts taking drugs.

verses.gurmukhi: ਅਗਣਤ ਪਾਉ ਪਸਾਰੀ ਰੁਲੇ ਅਹਾੜ ਵਿਚਿ ॥
verses.transliteration: aganat paau pasaaree rule ahaaR vich ||
verses.translation: Innumerable warriors are lying neglected in the battlefield, stretching their legs.

verses.gurmukhi: ਜਾਪੇ ਖੇਡ ਖਿਡਾਰੀ ਸੁੱਤੇ ਫਾਗ ਨੰੂ ॥੩੪॥
verses.transliteration: jaape khedd khiddaaree su'te faag na(n)oo ||34||
verses.translation: It seems that the revelers playing Holi are sleeping.34.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸ੍ਰਣਵਤ ਬੀਜ ਹਕਾਰੇ ਰਹਿੰਦੇ ਸੂਰਮੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: sranavat beej hakaare rahi(n)dhe soorame ||
verses.translation: Sranwat Beej called all the remaining warriors.

verses.gurmukhi: ਜੋਧੇ ਜੇਡ ਮੁਨਾਰੇ ਦਿੱਸਣ ਖੇਤ ਵਿਚਿ ॥
verses.transliteration: jodhe jedd munaare dhi'san khet vich ||
verses.translation: They seem like minarets in the battlefield.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸਭਨੀ ਦਸਤ ਉਭਾਰੇ ਤੇਗਾਂ ਧੂਹਿ ਕੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: sabhanee dhasat ubhaare tegaa(n) dhooh kai ||
verses.translation: All of them pulling their swords, raised their hands.

verses.gurmukhi: ਮਾਰੋ ਮਾਰ ਪੁਕਾਰੇ ਆਏ ਸਾਹਮਣੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: maaro maar pukaare aae saahamane ||
verses.translation: They came in front shouting “kill, kill”.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸੰਜਾਤੇ ਠਣਿਕਾਰੇ ਤੇਗੀਂ ਉੱਭਰੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa(n)jaate Thanikaare tegee(n) u'bhare ||
verses.translation: With the striking of swords on the armour, the clatter arises.

verses.gurmukhi: ਘਾੜ ਘੜਨਿ ਠਠਿਆਰੇ ਜਾਣਿ ਬਣਾਇ ਕੈ ॥੩੫॥
verses.transliteration: ghaaR ghaRan ThaThiaare jaan banai kai ||35||
verses.translation: It seems that the tinkers are fashioning the vessels with the blows of hammer.35.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸੱਟ ਪਈ ਜਮਧਾਣੀ ਦਲਾਂ ਮੁਕਾਬਲਾ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa'T piee jamadhaanee dhalaa(n) mukaabalaa ||
verses.translation: When the trumpet enveloped by the hide of the male buffalo, the vehicle of Yama, sounded, the armies attacked each other.

verses.gurmukhi: ਘੂਮਰ ਬਰਗ ਸਤਾਣੀ ਦਲ ਵਿਚਿ ਘੱਤਿਓ ॥
verses.transliteration: ghoomar barag sataanee dhal vich gha'tio ||
verses.translation: (The goddess) was the cause of flight and consternation in the battlefield.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸਣੇ ਤੁਰਾ ਪਲਾਣੀ ਡਿੱਗਣ ਸੂਰਮੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: sane turaa palaanee ddi'gan soorame ||
verses.translation: The warriors fall alongwith their horses and saddles.

verses.gurmukhi: ਉਠਿ ਉਠਿ ਮੰਗਣਿ ਪਾਣੀ ਘਾਇਲ ਘੂਮਦੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: auTh uTh ma(n)gan paanee ghail ghoomadhe ||
verses.translation: The wounded ones arise and ask for water while roaming.

verses.gurmukhi: ਏਵਡੁ ਮਾਰਿ ਵਿਹਾਣੀ ਉਪਰ ਰਾਕਸਾਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: evadd maar vihaanee upar raakasaa(n) ||
verses.translation: Such a great calamity fell on the demons.

verses.gurmukhi: ਬਿੱਜਲ ਜਿਉ ਝਰਲਾਣੀ ਉੱਠੀ ਦੇਵਤਾ ॥੩੬॥
verses.transliteration: bi'jal jiau jharalaanee u'Thee dhevataa ||36||
verses.translation: From this side the goddess rose like thundering lightning.36.

verses.gurmukhi: ਚੋਬੀ ਧਉਸ ਉਭਾਰੀ ਦਲਾਂ ਮੁਕਾਬਲਾ ॥
verses.transliteration: chobee dhaus ubhaaree dhalaa(n) mukaabalaa ||
verses.translation: The drummer sounded the trumpet and the armies attacked each other.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸਭੋ ਸੈਨਾ ਮਾਰੀ ਪਲ ਵਿਚਿ ਦਾਨਵੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: sabho sainaa maaree pal vich dhaanavee ||
verses.translation: All the army of the demons was killed in an instant.

verses.gurmukhi: ਦੁਰਗਾ ਦਾਨੋ ਮਾਰੇ ਰੋਹ ਬਢਾਇ ਕੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhuragaa dhaano maare roh baddai kai ||
verses.translation: Highly infuriated, Durga killed the demons.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸਿਰ ਵਿਚ ਤੇਗ ਵਗਾਈ ਸ੍ਰਣਵਤ ਬੀਜ ਦੇ ॥੩੭॥
verses.transliteration: sir vich teg vagaiee sranavat beej dhe ||37||
verses.translation: She struck the sword on the head of Sranwat Beej.37.

verses.gurmukhi: ਅਗਣਤ ਦਾਨੋ ਭਾਰੇ ਹੋਏ ਲੋਹੂਆ ॥
verses.transliteration: aganat dhaano bhaare hoe lohooaa ||
verses.translation: Innumerable mighty demons were steeped in blood.

verses.gurmukhi: ਜੋਧੇ ਜੇਡ ਮੁਨਾਰੇ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਖੇਤ ਦੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: jodhe jedd munaare a(n)dhar khet dhai ||
verses.translation: Those minarets-like demons in the battlefield

verses.gurmukhi: ਦੁਰਗਾ ਨੋ ਲਲਕਾਰੇ ਆਏ ਸਾਹਮਣੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhuragaa no lalakaare aae saahamane ||
verses.translation: They challenged Durga and came in front of her.

verses.gurmukhi: ਦੁਰਗਾ ਸਭ ਸੰਘਾਰੇ ਰਾਕਸ ਆਂਵਦੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhuragaa sabh sa(n)ghaare raakas aa(n)vadhe ||
verses.translation: Durga killed all the coming demons.

verses.gurmukhi: ਰਤੂ ਦੇ ਪਰਨਾਲੇ ਤਿਨ ਤੇ ਭੁਇ ਪਏ ॥
verses.transliteration: ratoo dhe paranaale tin te bhui pe ||
verses.translation: From their bodies the drains of blood fell on the ground.

verses.gurmukhi: ਉੱਠੇ ਕਾਰਣਿਆਰੇ ਰਾਕਸ ਹੜਹੜਾਇ ॥੩੮॥
verses.transliteration: au'The kaaraniaare raakas haRahaRai ||38||
verses.translation: Some of the active demons arise out of them laughingly.38.

verses.gurmukhi: ਧਗਾ ਸੰਗਲੀਆਲੀ ਸੰਘਰ ਵਾਇਆ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhagaa sa(n)galeeaalee sa(n)ghar vaiaa ||
verses.translation: The enchained trumpets and bugles sounded.

verses.gurmukhi: ਬਰਛੀ ਬੁੰਬਲੀਆਲੀ ਸੂਰੇ ਸੰਘਰੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: barachhee bu(n)baleeaalee soore sa(n)ghare ||
verses.translation: The warriors fought with daggers bedecked with tassels.

verses.gurmukhi: ਭੇੜਿ ਮਚਿਆ ਬੀਰਾਲੀ ਦੁਰਗਾ ਦਾਨਵੀਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: bheR machiaa beeraalee dhuragaa dhaanavee(n) ||
verses.translation: The war of bravery was waged between Durga and demos.

verses.gurmukhi: ਮਾਰ ਮਚੀ ਮੁਹਰਾਲੀ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਖੇਤ ਦੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: maar machee muharaalee a(n)dhar khet dhai ||
verses.translation: There had been extreme destruction in the battlefield.

verses.gurmukhi: ਜਣ ਨਟ ਲੱਥੇ ਛਾਲੀ ਢੋਲਿ ਬਜਾਇ ਕੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: jan naT la'the chhaalee ddol bajai kai ||
verses.translation: It appears that the actors, sounding their drum, have jumped into the war-arena.

verses.gurmukhi: ਲੋਹੂ ਫਾਥੀ ਜਾਲੀ ਲੋਥੀ ਜਮਧੜੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: lohoo faathee jaalee lothee jamadhaRee ||
verses.translation: The dagger penetrated in the corpse seems like a blood stained fish entrapped in the net.

verses.gurmukhi: ਘਣ ਵਿਚਿ ਜਿਉ ਛੰਛਾਲੀ ਤੇਗਾਂ ਹੱਸੀਆਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: ghan vich jiau chha(n)chhaalee tegaa(n) ha'seeaa(n) ||
verses.translation: The swords glistened like the lightning in the clouds.

verses.gurmukhi: ਘੁੰਮਰਿਆਰ ਸਿਆਲੀ ਬਣੀਆਂ ਕੇਜਮਾਂ ॥੩੯॥
verses.transliteration: ghu(n)mariaar siaalee baneeaa(n) kejamaa(n) ||39||
verses.translation: The swords have covered (the battlefield) like the winter-fog.39.

verses.gurmukhi: ਧਗਾ ਸੂਲੀ ਬਜਾਈਆਂ ਦਲਾਂ ਮੁਕਾਬਲਾ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhagaa soolee bajaieeaa(n) dhalaa(n) mukaabalaa ||
verses.translation: The trumpets were sounded with the beating of drum-stick and the armies attacked each other.

verses.gurmukhi: ਧੂਹਿ ਮਿਆਨੋ ਲਈਆਂ ਜੁਆਨੀ ਸੂਰਮੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhooh miaano lieeaa(n) juaanee sooramee ||
verses.translation: The youthful warriors pulled out their swords from their scabbards.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸ੍ਰਣਵਤ ਬੀਜ ਬਧਾਈਆਂ ਅਗਣਤ ਸੂਰਤਾਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: sranavat beej badhaieeaa(n) aganat soorataa(n) ||
verses.translation: Sranwat Beej increased himself into innumerable forms.

verses.gurmukhi: ਦੁਰਗਾ ਸਉਹੇਂ ਆਈਆਂ ਰੋਹ ਬਢਾਇ ਕੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhuragaa sauhe(n) aaieeaa(n) roh baddai kai ||
verses.translation: Which came in front of Durga, highly enraged.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸਭਨੀ ਆਣ ਵਗਾਈਆਂ ਤੇਗਾਂ ਧੂਹਿ ਕੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: sabhanee aan vagaieeaa(n) tegaa(n) dhooh kai ||
verses.translation: All of them pulled out their swords and struck.

verses.gurmukhi: ਦੁਰਗਾ ਸਭ ਬਚਾਈਆਂ ਢਾਲ ਸੰਭਾਲ ਕੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhuragaa sabh bachaieeaa(n) ddaal sa(n)bhaal kai ||
verses.translation: Durga saved herself from all, holding her shield carefully.

verses.gurmukhi: ਦੇਵੀ ਆਪ ਚਲਾਈਆਂ ਤਕਿ ਤਕਿ ਦਾਨਵੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhevee aap chalaieeaa(n) tak tak dhaanavee ||
verses.translation: The goddess herself then struck her sword looking carefully toward the demons.

verses.gurmukhi: ਲੋਹੂ ਨਾਲਿ ਡੁਬਾਈਆਂ ਤੇਗਾਂ ਨੰਗੀਆਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: lohoo naal ddubaieeaa(n) tegaa(n) na(n)geeaa(n) ||
verses.translation: She steeped her naked swords in blood.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸਾਰਸੁਤੀ ਜਨੁ ਨਾਈਆਂ ਮਿਲ ਕੈ ਦੇਵੀਆਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: saarasutee jan naieeaa(n) mil kai dheveeaa(n) ||
verses.translation: It appeared that the goddesses gathering together, took their bath in river Saraswati.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸਭੇ ਮਾਰ ਗਿਰਾਈਆਂ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਖੇਤ ਦੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: sabhe maar giraieeaa(n) a(n)dhar khet dhai ||
verses.translation: The goddess hath killed and thrown on the ground in the battlefield (all the forms of Sranwat Beej).

verses.gurmukhi: ਤਿੱਦੂੰ ਫੇਰਿ ਸਵਾਈਆਂ ਹੋਈਆਂ ਸੂਰਤਾਂ ॥੪੦॥
verses.transliteration: ti'dhoo(n) fer savaieeaa(n) hoieeaa(n) soorataa(n) ||40||
verses.translation: Immediately then the forms again increased greatly.40.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸੂਰੀ ਸੰਘਰਿ ਰਚਿਆ ਢੋਲ ਸੰਖ ਨਗਾਰੇ ਵਾਇ ਕੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: sooree sa(n)ghar rachiaa ddol sa(n)kh nagaare vai kai ||
verses.translation: Sounding their drums, conches and trumpets, the warriors have begun the war.

verses.gurmukhi: ਚੰਡ ਚਿਤਾਰੀ ਕਾਲਕਾ ਮਨਿ ਬਾਹਲਾ ਰੋਸ ਬਢਾਇ ਕੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: cha(n)dd chitaaree kaalakaa man baahalaa ros baddai kai ||
verses.translation: Chandi, being highly enraged, remembered Kali in her mind.

verses.gurmukhi: ਨਿਕਲੀ ਮੱਥਾ ਫੋੜਿ ਕੈ ਜਨ ਫਤੇ ਨੀਸਾਣ ਬਜਾਇ ਕੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: nikalee ma'thaa foR kai jan fate neesaan bajai kai ||
verses.translation: She came out shattering the forehead of Chandi, sounding the trumpet and flying flag of victory.

verses.gurmukhi: ਜਾਗ ਸੁ ਜੰਮੀ ਜੁਧ ਨੋ ਜਰਵਾਣਾ ਜਣੁ ਮਰੜਾਇ ਕੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: jaag su ja(n)mee judh no jaravaanaa jan maraRai kai ||
verses.translation: On manifesting herself, she marched for war, like Bir Bhadra manifesting from Shiva.

verses.gurmukhi: ਦਲ ਵਿਚਿ ਘੇਰਾ ਘੱਤਿਆ ਜਣ ਸੀਂਹ ਤੁਰਿਆ ਗਣਿਣਾਇ ਕੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhal vich gheraa gha'tiaa jan see(n)h turiaa ganinai kai ||
verses.translation: The battlefield was surrounded by her and she seemed moving like a roaring lion.

verses.gurmukhi: ਆਪ ਵਿਸੂਲਾ ਹੋਇਆ ਤਿਹੁ ਲੋਕਾਂ ਤੇ ਖੁਨਸਾਇ ਕੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: aap visoolaa hoiaa tih lokaa(n) te khunasai kai ||
verses.translation: (The demon-king) himself was in great anguish, while exhibiting his anger over the three worlds.

verses.gurmukhi: ਰੋਹ ਸਿਧਾਇਆਂ ਚਕ੍ਰ ਪਾਨ ਕਰ ਨਿੰਦਾ ਖੜਗ ਉਠਾਇ ਕੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: roh sidhaiaa(n) chakr paan kar ni(n)dhaa khaRag uThai kai ||
verses.translation: Durga, being enraged, hath marched, holding her disc in her hand and raising her sword.

verses.gurmukhi: ਅਗੈ ਰਾਕਸ ਬੈਠੇ ਰੋਹਲੇ ਤੀਰੀ ਤੇਗੀ ਛਹਬਰ ਲਾਇ ਕੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: agai raakas baiThe rohale teeree tegee chhahabar lai kai ||
verses.translation: There before her there were infuriated demons, she caught and knocked down the demons.

verses.gurmukhi: ਪਕੜ ਪਛਾੜੇ ਰਾਕਸਾਂ ਦਲ ਦੈਤਾਂ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਜਾਇ ਕੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: pakaR pachhaaRe raakasaa(n) dhal dhaitaa(n) a(n)dhar jai kai ||
verses.translation: Going within the forces of demons, she caught and knocked down the demons.

verses.gurmukhi: ਬਹੁ ਕੇਸੀ ਪਕੜਿ ਪਛਾੜਿਅਨਿ ਤਿਨ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਧੂਮ ਰਚਾਇ ਕੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: bahu kesee pakaR pachhaaRian tin a(n)dhar dhoom rachai kai ||
verses.translation: She threw down by catching them from their hair and raising a tumult among their forces.

verses.gurmukhi: ਬਡੇ ਬਡੇ ਚੁਣ ਸੂਰਮੇ ਗਹਿ ਕੋਟੀ ਦਏ ਚਲਾਇ ਕੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: badde badde chun soorame geh koTee dhe chalai kai ||
verses.translation: She picked up mighty fighters by catching them with the corner of her bow and throwing them

verses.gurmukhi: ਰਣ ਕਾਲੀ ਗੁੱਸਾ ਖਾਇ ਕੈ ॥੪੧॥
verses.transliteration: ran kaalee gu'saa khai kai ||41||
verses.translation: In her fury, Kali hath done this in the battlefield.41.

verses.gurmukhi: ਦੁਹਾ ਕੰਧਾਰਾ ਮੁਿਹ ਜੁੜੇ ਅਣੀਆਰਾ ਚੋਈਆਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhuhaa ka(n)dhaaraa mueh juRe aneeaaraa choieeaa(n) ||
verses.translation: Both the armies are facing each other and the blood is dripping from the tips of arrows.

verses.gurmukhi: ਧੂਹਿ ਕਿਰਪਾਣਾਂ ਤਿੱਖੀਆ ਨਾਲ ਲੋਹੂ ਧੋਈਆਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhooh kirapaanaa(n) ti'kheeaa naal lohoo dhoieeaa(n) ||
verses.translation: Pulling the sharp swords, they have been washed with blood.

verses.gurmukhi: ਹੂਰਾਂ ਸ੍ਰਣਵਤ ਬੀਜ ਨੰੂ ਘਤਿ ਘੇਿਰ ਖਲੋਈਆਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: hooraa(n) sranavat beej na(n)oo ghat ghir khaloieeaa(n) ||
verses.translation: The heavenly damsels (houris), surrounding Sranwat Beej, are standing

verses.gurmukhi: ਲਾੜਾ ਵੇਖਣਿ ਲਾੜੀਆਂ ਚਉਗਿਰਦੈ ਹੋਈਆਂ ॥੪੨॥
verses.transliteration: laaRaa vekhan laaReeaa(n) chaugiradhai hoieeaa(n) ||42||
verses.translation: Like the brides surrounding the bridegroom in order to see him.42.

verses.gurmukhi: ਚੋਬੀ ਧਉਸੀ ਪਾਈਆਂ ਦਲਾਂ ਮੁਕਾਬਲਾ ॥
verses.transliteration: chobee dhausee paieeaa(n) dhalaa(n) mukaabalaa ||
verses.translation: The drummer beat the trumpet and armies attacked each other.

verses.gurmukhi: ਦਸਤੀ ਧੂਹ ਨਚਾਈਆਂ ਤੇਗਾਂ ਤਿਖੀਆਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhasatee dhooh nachaieeaa(n) tegaa(n) tikheeaa(n) ||

verses.gurmukhi: ਸੂਰਿਆਂ ਦੇ ਤਨ ਲਾਈਆਂ ਗੋਸ਼ਤ ਗਿੱਧੀਆਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: sooriaa(n) dhe tan laieeaa(n) goshat gi'dheeaa(n) ||
verses.translation: With their hands they pulled the naked sword and caused their dance.

verses.gurmukhi: ਬਿੱਧਣ ਰਾਤੀ ਆਈਆਂ ਮਰਦਾਂ ਘੋੜਿਆਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: bi'dhan raatee aaieeaa(n) maradhaa(n) ghoRiaa(n) ||
verses.translation: These devourers of meat were struck on the bodies of the warriors.

verses.gurmukhi: ਜੋਗਣੀਆਂ ਮਿਲਿ ਧਾਈਆਂ ਲੋਹੂ ਭੱਖਣਾ ॥
verses.transliteration: joganeeaa(n) mil dhaieeaa(n) lohoo bha'khanaa ||
verses.translation: The nights of agony have come for the men and horses.

verses.gurmukhi: ਫਉਜਾਂ ਮਾਰਿ ਹਟਾਈਅਥ ਦੇਵਾਂ ਦਾਨਵਾਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: faujaa(n) maar haTaieeath dhevaa(n) dhaanavaa(n) ||
verses.translation: The Yoginis have come together speedily in order to drink the blood.

verses.gurmukhi: ਭਜਦੀ ਕਥਾ ਸੁਣਾਈਆਂ ਰਾਜੇ ਸੁੰਭ ਥੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: bhajadhee kathaa sunaieeaa(n) raaje su(n)bh thai ||
verses.translation: They told the story of their repulsion before the king Sumbh.

verses.gurmukhi: ਭੁਈਂ ਨ ਪਉਣੈ ਪਾਈਆਂ ਬੂੰਦਾ ਰਕਤ ਦੀਆਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: bhuiee(n) na paunai paieeaa(n) boo(n)dhaa rakat dheeaa(n) ||
verses.translation: The drops of blood (of Sranwat Beej) could not fall on the earth.

verses.gurmukhi: ਕਾਲੀ ਖੇਤ ਖਪਾਈਆਂ ਸਭੈ ਸੂਰਤਾਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: kaalee khet khapaieeaa(n) sabhai soorataa(n) ||
verses.translation: Kali destroyed all the manifestations of (Sranwat Beej) in the battlefield.

verses.gurmukhi: ਬਹੁਤੀ ਸਿਰੀ ਵਿਹਾਈਆਂ ਘੜੀਆਂ ਕਾਲ ਕੀਆ ॥
verses.transliteration: bahutee siree vihaieeaa(n) ghaReeaa(n) kaal keeaa ||
verses.translation: The last moments of death came over the heads of many fighters.

verses.gurmukhi: ਜਾਣਿ ਨ ਜਾਏ ਮਾਈਆਂ ਜੂਝੇ ਸੂਰਮੇ ॥੪੩॥
verses.transliteration: jaan na jaae maieeaa(n) joojhe soorame ||43||
verses.translation: The brave fighters could not even be recognized by their mothers, who gave birth to them.43.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸੁੰਭ ਸੁਣੀ ਕਰਹਾਲੀ ਸ੍ਰਣਵਤ ਬੀਜ ਦੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: su(n)bh sunee karahaalee sranavat beej dhee ||
verses.translation: Sumbh heard the bad news about the death of Sranwat Beej

verses.gurmukhi: ਰਣ ਵਿਚਿ ਕਿਨੈ ਨ ਝਾਲੀ ਦੁਰਗਾ ਆਂਵਦੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: ran vich kinai na jhaalee dhuragaa aa(n)vadhee ||
verses.translation: And that none could withstand the marching Durga in the battlefield.

verses.gurmukhi: ਬਹੁਤੇ ਬੀਰ ਜਟਾਲੀ ਉਠੇ ਆਖਿ ਕੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: bahute beer jaTaalee uThe aakh kai ||
verses.translation: Many brave fighters with matted hair got up saing

verses.gurmukhi: ਚੋਟਾ ਪਾਨ ਤਬਾਲੀ ਜਾਸਨ ਜੁੱਧ ਨੰੂ ॥
verses.transliteration: choTaa paan tabaalee jaasan ju'dh na(n)oo ||
verses.translation: That drummers should sound the drums because they would go for war.

verses.gurmukhi: ਥਰਿ ਥਰਿ ਪ੍ਰਿਥਮੀ ਚਾਲੀ ਦਲਾਂ ਚੜੰਦਿਆਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: thar thar pirathamee chaalee dhalaa(n) chaRa(n)dhiaa(n) ||
verses.translation: When the armies marched, the earth trembled

verses.gurmukhi: ਨਾਉ ਜਿਵੇ ਹੈ ਹਾਲੀ ਸਹੁ ਦਰੀਆਉ ਵਿਚਿ ॥
verses.transliteration: naau jive hai haalee sahu dhareeaau vich ||
verses.translation: Like the shaking boat, which is still in the river.

verses.gurmukhi: ਧੂੜਿ ਉਤਾਹਾਂ ਘਾਲੀ ਛੜੀ ਤੁਰੰਗਮਾਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhooR utaahaa(n) ghaalee chhaRee tura(n)gamaa(n) ||
verses.translation: The dust arose with the hooves of the horses

verses.gurmukhi: ਜਾਣਿ ਪੁਕਾਰੂ ਚਾਲੀ ਧਰਤੀ ਇੰਦ੍ਰ ਥੈ ॥੪੪॥
verses.transliteration: jaan pukaaroo chaalee dharatee i(n)dhr thai ||44||
verses.translation: And it seemed that the earth is going to Indra for a complaint.44.

verses.gurmukhi: ਆਹਰਿ ਮਿਲਿਆ ਆਹਰੀਆਂ ਸੈਣ ਸੂਰਿਆਂ ਸਾਜੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: aahar miliaa aahareeaa(n) sain sooriaa(n) saajee ||
verses.translation: The willing workers got engaged in work and as warriors they equipped the army.

verses.gurmukhi: ਚੱਲੇ ਸਉਹੇ ਦੁਰਗਸਾਹ ਜਣ ਕਾਬੈ ਹਾਜੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: cha'le sauhe dhuragasaeh jan kaabai haajee ||
verses.translation: They marched in front of Durga, like pilgrims going for Haj to Kaabah (Mecca).

verses.gurmukhi: ਤੀਰੀ ਤੇਗੀ ਜਮਧੜੀ ਰਣਿ ਵੰਡੀ ਭਾਜੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: teeree tegee jamadhaRee ran va(n)ddee bhaajee ||
verses.translation: They are inviting the warriors in the battlefield through the medium of arrows, swords and daggers.

verses.gurmukhi: ਇਕ ਘਾਇਲ ਘੁਮਨਿ ਸੂਰਮੇ ਜਣੁ ਮਕਤਬ ਕਾਜੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: eik ghail ghuman soorame jan makatab kaajee ||
verses.translation: Some wounded warriors are swinging like the Quadis in the school, reciting the holy Quran.

verses.gurmukhi: ਇਕ ਬੀਰ ਪਰੋਤੇ ਬਰਛੀਏ ਜਿਉ ਝੁਕ ਪਉਨ ਨਿਵਾਜੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: eik beer parote barachhe'ee jiau jhuk paun nivaajee ||
verses.translation: Some brave fighters are pierced by daggers and lining like a devout Muslim performing prayer.

verses.gurmukhi: ਇਕ ਦੁਰਗਾ ਸਉਹੇ ਖੁਨਸ ਕੈ ਖੁਣਸਾਇਨ ਤਾਜੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: eik dhuragaa sauhe khunas kai khunasain taajee ||
verses.translation: Some go in front of Durga in great fury by inciting their malicious horses.

verses.gurmukhi: ਇਕ ਧਾਵਨ ਦੁਰਗਾ ਸਾਮ੍ਹਣੇ ਜਿਉ ਭੁਖਿਆਏ ਪਾਜੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: eik dhaavan dhuragaa saamhane jiau bhukhiaae paajee ||
verses.translation: Some run in front of Durga like the hungry scoundrels

verses.gurmukhi: ਕਦੇ ਨ ਰੱਜੇ ਜੁਝ ਤੇ ਰਜਿ ਹੋਏ ਰਾਜੀ ॥੪੫॥
verses.transliteration: kadhe na ra'je jujh te raj hoe raajee ||45||
verses.translation: Who had never been satisfied in the war, but now they are satiated and pleased.45.

verses.gurmukhi: ਬੱਜੇ ਸੰਗਲੀਆਲੇ ਸੰਘਰ ਡੋਹਰੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: ba'je sa(n)galeeaale sa(n)ghar ddohare ||
verses.translation: The enchained double trumpets sounded.

verses.gurmukhi: ਡਹੇ ਜੁ ਖੇਤ ਜਟਾਲੇ ਹਾਠਾਂ ਜੋੜਿ ਕੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: ddahe ju khet jaTaale haaThaa(n) joR kai ||
verses.translation: Gathering together in ranks, the warriors with matted hair are engaged in war in the battlefield.

verses.gurmukhi: ਨੇਜੇ ਬੰਬਲੀਆਲੇ ਦਿੱਸਨ ਓਰੜੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: neje ba(n)baleeaale dhi'san oraRe ||
verses.translation: The lances bedecked with tassels seem leaning

verses.gurmukhi: ਚੱਲੇ ਜਾਣ ਜਟਾਲੇ ਨਾਵਣ ਗੰਗ ਨੂੰ ॥੪੬॥
verses.transliteration: cha'le jaan jaTaale naavan ga(n)g noo(n) ||46||
verses.translation: Like the hermits with matted locks going towards the Ganges for taking a bath.46.

verses.gurmukhi: ਦੁਰਗਾ ਅਤੈ ਦਾਨਵੀ ਸੂਲ ਹੋਈਆਂ ਕੰਗਾ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhuragaa atai dhaanavee sool hoieeaa(n) ka(n)gaa ||
verses.translation: The forces of Durga and demons are piercing each other like sharp thorns.

verses.gurmukhi: ਵਾਛੜ ਘੱਤੀ ਸੂਰਿਆਂ ਵਿਚ ਖੇਤ ਖਤੰਗਾਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: vaachhaR gha'tee sooriaa(n) vich khet khata(n)gaa(n) ||
verses.translation: The warriors showered arrows in the battlefield.

verses.gurmukhi: ਧੂਹਿ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾਣਾ ਤਿੱਖੀਆਂ ਬਢ ਲਾਹਨਿ ਅੰਗਾਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhooh kirapaanaa ti'kheeaa(n) badd laahan a(n)gaa(n) ||
verses.translation: Pulling their sharp swords, they chop the limbs.

verses.gurmukhi: ਪਹਲਾ ਦਲਾਂ ਮਿਲੰਦਿਆਂ ਭੇੜੁ ਪਾਇਆ ਨਿਹੰਗਾਂ ॥੪੭॥
verses.transliteration: pahalaa dhalaa(n) mila(n)dhiaa(n) bheR paiaa niha(n)gaa(n) ||47||
verses.translation: When the forces met, at first there was war with swords.47.

verses.gurmukhi: ਓਰੜ ਫਉਜਾਂ ਆਈਆਂ ਬੀਰ ਚੜੇ ਕੰਧਾਰੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: oraR faujaa(n) aaieeaa(n) beer chaRe ka(n)dhaaree ||
verses.translation: The forces came in great numbers and the ranks of warriors marched forward

verses.gurmukhi: ਸੜਕ ਮਿਆਨੋ ਕਢੀਆਂ ਤਿੱਖੀਆ ਤਰਵਾਰੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: saRak miaano kaddeeaa(n) ti'kheeaa taravaaree ||
verses.translation: They pulled their sharp swords from their scabbards.

verses.gurmukhi: ਕੜਕ ਉਠੇ ਰਣ ਮੱਚਿਆ ਵੱਡੇ ਹੰਕਾਰੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: kaRak uThe ran ma'chiaa va'dde ha(n)kaaree ||
verses.translation: With the blazing of the war, the great egoist warriors shouted loudly.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸਿਰ ਧੜ ਬਾਹਾਂ ਗਨ ਲੇ ਫੁਲ ਜੇਹੈ ਬਾੜੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: sir dhaR baahaa(n) gan le ful jehai baaRee ||
verses.translation: The pieces of head, trunk and arms look like garden-flowers.

verses.gurmukhi: ਜਾਪੇ ਕਟੇ ਬਾਢੀਆਂ ਰੁਖ ਚੰਦਨਿ ਆਰੀ ॥੪੮॥
verses.transliteration: jaape kaTe baaddeeaa(n) rukh cha(n)dhan aaree ||48||
verses.translation: And (the bodies) appear like the trees of sandalwood cut and sawed by the carpenters.48.

verses.gurmukhi: ਦੁਹਾ ਕੰਧਾਰਾ ਮੁਹਿ ਜੁੜੇ ਜਾ ਸੱਟ ਪਈ ਖਰਵਾਰ ਕਉ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhuhaa ka(n)dhaaraa muh juRe jaa sa'T piee kharavaar kau ||
verses.translation: When the trumpet, enveloped by the hide of a donkey, was beaten, both the forces faced each other.

verses.gurmukhi: ਤਕ ਤਕ ਕੈਬਰਿ ਦੁਰਗਸਾਹ ਤਕ ਮਾਰੇ ਭਲੇ ਜੁਝਾਰ ਕਉ ॥
verses.transliteration: tak tak kaibar dhuragasaeh tak maare bhale jujhaar kau ||
verses.translation: Looking at the warriors, Durga pointedly shot her arrows on the brave fighters.

verses.gurmukhi: ਪੈਦਲ ਮਾਰੇ ਹਾਥੀਆਂ ਸੰਗਿ ਰਥ ਗਿਰੇ ਅਸਵਾਰ ਕਉ ॥
verses.transliteration: paidhal maare haatheeaa(n) sa(n)g rath gire asavaar kau ||
verses.translation: The warriors on foot were killed, the elephants were killed alongwith the fall of the chariots and horse- riders.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸੋਹਨ ਸੰਜਾ ਬਾਗੜਾ ਜਣੁ ਲੱਗੇ ਫੱੁਲ ਅਨਾਰ ਕਉ ॥
verses.transliteration: sohan sa(n)jaa baagaRaa jan la'ge fa'ul anaar kau ||
verses.translation: The tips of arrows penetrated in the armour like the flowers on pomegranate-plants.

verses.gurmukhi: ਗੱੁਸੇ ਆਈ ਕਾਲਕਾ ਹਥਿ ਸੱਜੇ ਲੈ ਤਲਵਾਰ ਕਉ ॥
verses.transliteration: ga'use aaiee kaalakaa hath sa'je lai talavaar kau ||
verses.translation: The goddess Kali got enraged, holding her sword in her right hand

verses.gurmukhi: ਏਦੂ ਪਾਰਉ ਓਤ ਪਾਰ ਹਰਨਾਕਸਿ ਕਈ ਹਜਾਰ ਕਉ ॥
verses.transliteration: edhoo paarau ot paar haranaakas kiee hajaar kau ||
verses.translation: She destroyed several thousand demons (Hiranayakashipus) from this end of the field to the other end.

verses.gurmukhi: ਜਿਣ ਇੱਕਾ ਰਹੀ ਕੰਧਾਰ ਕਉ ॥
verses.transliteration: jin i'kaa rahee ka(n)dhaar kau ||
verses.translation: The only one is conquering the army

verses.gurmukhi: ਸਦ ਰਹਮਤ ਤੇਰੇ ਵਾਰ ਕਉ ॥੪੯॥
verses.transliteration: sadh rahamat tere vaar kau ||49||
verses.translation: O goddess! Hail, hail to Thy blow.49.

verses.gurmukhi: ਦੁਹਾ ਕੰਧਾਰਾ ਮੁਹਿ ਜੁੜੇ ਸੱਟ ਪਈ ਜਮਧਾਣ ਕਉ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhuhaa ka(n)dhaaraa muh juRe sa'T piee jamadhaan kau ||
verses.translation: The trumpet, enveloped by the hide of the male buffalo, the vehicle of Yama, was beaten and both the armies faced each other.

verses.gurmukhi: ਤਦ ਖਿੰਗ ਨਸੁੰਭ ਨਚਾਇਆ ਡਾਲਿ ਉਪਰਿ ਬਰਗਸਤਾਣ ਕਉ ॥
verses.transliteration: tadh khi(n)g nasu(n)bh nachaiaa ddaal upar baragasataan kau ||
verses.translation: Then Nisumbh caused the horse to dance, putting on his back the saddle-armour.

verses.gurmukhi: ਫੜੀ ਬਿਲੰਦ ਮੰਗਾਇਉਸੁ ਫਰਮਾਇਸ ਕਰਿ ਮੁਲਤਾਨ ਕਉ ॥
verses.transliteration: faRee bila(n)dh ma(n)gaiaus faramais kar mulataan kau ||
verses.translation: She held the big bow, which was caused to be brought on order form Musltan.

verses.gurmukhi: ਗੱੁਸੇ ਆਈ ਸਾਹਮਣੇ ਰਣ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਘੱਤਣ ਘਾਣ ਕਉ ॥
verses.transliteration: ga'use aaiee saahamane ran a(n)dhar gha'tan ghaan kau ||
verses.translation: In her fury, she came in front in order to fill the battlefield with the mud of blood and fat.

verses.gurmukhi: ਅਗੈ ਤੇਗ ਵਗਾਈ ਦੁਰਗਸਾਹ ਬਢ ਸੁੰਭਨ ਬਹੀ ਪਲਾਣ ਕਉ ॥
verses.transliteration: agai teg vagaiee dhuragasaeh badd su(n)bhan bahee palaan kau ||
verses.translation: Durga struck the sword in front of her, cutting the demon-king, penetrated through the horse-saddle.

verses.gurmukhi: ਰੜਕੀ ਜਾਇ ਕੈ ਧਰਤ ਕਉ ਬੱਢ ਪਾਖਰ ਬੱਢ ਕਿਕਾਣ ਕਉ ॥
verses.transliteration: raRakee jai kai dharat kau ba'dd paakhar ba'dd kikaan kau ||
verses.translation: Then it penetrated further and struck the earth after cutting the saddle-armour and the horse.

verses.gurmukhi: ਬੀਰ ਪਲਾਣੋ ਡਿੱਗਿਆ ਕਰਿ ਸਿਜਦਾ ਸੁੰਭ ਸੁਜਾਣ ਕਉ ॥
verses.transliteration: beer palaano ddi'giaa kar sijadhaa su(n)bh sujaan kau ||
verses.translation: The great hero (Nisumbh) fell down from the horse-saddle, offering salutation to the wise Sumbh.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸਾਬਾਸ ਸਲੋਣੇ ਖਾਨ ਕਉ ॥
verses.transliteration: saabaas salone khaan kau ||
verses.translation: Hail, hail, to the winsome chieftain (Khan).

verses.gurmukhi: ਸਦ ਸਾਬਾਸ ਤੇਰੇ ਤਾਣ ਕਉ ॥
verses.transliteration: sadh saabaas tere taan kau ||
verses.translation: Hail, hail, ever to thy strength.

verses.gurmukhi: ਤਾਰੀਫਾਂ ਪਾਨ ਚਬਾਨ ਕਉ ॥
verses.transliteration: taareefaa(n) paan chabaan kau ||
verses.translation: Praises are offered for the chewing of betel.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸਦ ਰਹਮਤ ਕੈਫਾਂ ਖਾਣ ਕਉ ॥
verses.transliteration: sadh rahamat kaifaa(n) khaan kau ||
verses.translation: Hail, hail to thy addiction.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸਦ ਰਹਮਤ ਤੁਰੇ ਨਚਾਣ ਕਉ ॥੫੦॥
verses.transliteration: sadh rahamat ture nachaan kau ||50||
verses.translation: Hail hail, to thy horse-control.50.

verses.gurmukhi: ਦੁਰਗਾ ਅਤੈ ਦਾਨਵੀ ਗਹ ਸੰਘਰਿ ਕੱਥੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhuragaa atai dhaanavee geh sa(n)ghar ka'the ||
verses.translation: Durga and demons sounded their trumpets, in the remarkable war.

verses.gurmukhi: ਓਰੜ ਉੱਠੇ ਸੂਰਮੇ ਆ ਡਾਹੇ ਮੱਥੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: oraR u'The soorame aa ddaahe ma'the ||
verses.translation: The warriors arose in great numbers and have come to fight.

verses.gurmukhi: ਕੱਟ ਤੁਫੰਗੀ ਕੈਬਰੀ ਦਲ ਗਾਹਿ ਨਿੱਕਥੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: ka'T tufa(n)gee kaibaree dhal gaeh ni'kathe ||
verses.translation: They have come to tread through the forces in order to destroy (the enemy) with guns and arrows.

verses.gurmukhi: ਦੇਖਣਿ ਜੰਗ ਫਰੇਸ਼ਤੇ ਅਸਮਾਨੋ ਲੱਥੇ ॥੫੧॥
verses.transliteration: dhekhan ja(n)g fareshate asamaano la'the ||51||
verses.translation: The angels come down (to the earth) from the sky in order to see the war.51.

verses.gurmukhi: ਦੁਹਾ ਕੰਧਾਰਾਂ ਮੁਹ ਜੁੜੇ ਦਲ ਘੁਰੇ ਨਗਾਰੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhuhaa ka(n)dhaaraa(n) muh juRe dhal ghure nagaare ||
verses.translation: The trumpets have sounded in the army and both the forces face each other.

verses.gurmukhi: ਓਰੜ ਆਏ ਸੂਰਮੇ ਸਿਰਦਾਰ ਅਣਿਆਰੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: oraR aae soorame siradhaar aniaare ||
verses.translation: The chief and brave warriors swayed in the field.

verses.gurmukhi: ਲੈ ਕੇ ਤੇਗਾਂ ਬਰਛੀਆਂ ਹਥਿਆਰ ਉਭਾਰੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: lai ke tegaa(n) barachheeaa(n) hathiaar ubhaare ||
verses.translation: They raised their weapons including the swords and daggers.

verses.gurmukhi: ਟੋਪ ਪਟੇਲਾ ਪਾਖਰਾਂ ਗਲਿ ਸੰਜ ਸਵਾਰੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: Top paTelaa paakharaa(n) gal sa(n)j savaare ||
verses.translation: They have bedecked themselves with helmets on their heads, and armour around their necks alongwith their horse-sddles with belts.

verses.gurmukhi: ਲੈ ਕੇ ਬਰਛੀ ਦੁਰਗਸਾਹ ਬਹੁ ਦਾਨਵ ਮਾਰੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: lai ke barachhee dhuragasaeh bahu dhaanav maare ||
verses.translation: Durga holding her dagger, killed many demons.

verses.gurmukhi: ਚੜੇ ਰਥੀ ਗਜ ਘੋੜਿਈ ਮਾਰ ਭੁਇ ਤੇ ਡਾਰੇ ॥
verses.transliteration: chaRe rathee gaj ghoRiee maar bhui te ddaare ||
verses.translation: She killed and threw those on the round who were riding chariots, elephants and horses.

verses.gurmukhi: ਜਾਣ ਹਲਵਾਈ ਸੀਖ ਨਾਲ ਵਿੰਨ੍ਹ ਵੜੇ ਉਤਾਰੇ ॥੫੨॥
verses.transliteration: jaan halavaiee seekh naal vi(n)nh vaRe utaare ||52||
verses.translation: It appear that the confectioner has cooked small round cakes of grounded pulse, piercing them with a spike.52.

verses.gurmukhi: ਦੁਹਾਂ ਕੰਧਾਰਾਂ ਮੁਹਿ ਜੁੜੇ ਨਾਲ ਧਉਸਾ ਭਾਰੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhuhaa(n) ka(n)dhaaraa(n) muh juRe naal dhausaa bhaaree ||
verses.translation: Alongwith the sounding of the large trumpet, both the forces faced each other.

verses.gurmukhi: ਲਈ ਭਗਉਤੀ ਦੁਰਗਸਾਹ ਵਰ ਜਾਗਨ ਭਾਰੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: liee bhagautee dhuragasaeh var jaagan bhaaree ||
verses.translation: Durga held out her sword, appearing like great lustrous fire

verses.gurmukhi: ਲਾਈ ਰਾਜੇ ਸੁੰਭ ਨੋ ਰਤੁ ਪੀਐ ਪਿਆਰੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: laiee raaje su(n)bh no rat peeaai piaaree ||
verses.translation: She struck it on the king Sumbh and this lovely weapon drinks blood.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸੁੰਭ ਪਲਾਣੋ ਡਿੱਗਿਆ ਉਪਮਾ ਬੀਚਾਰੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: su(n)bh palaano ddi'giaa upamaa beechaaree ||
verses.translation: Sumbh fell down from the saddle for which the following simile hath been thought.

verses.gurmukhi: ਡੁਬ ਰਤੁ ਨਾਲਹੁ ਨਿਕਲੀ ਬਰਛੀ ਦੁਧਾਰੀ ॥
verses.transliteration: ddub rat naalahu nikalee barachhee dhudhaaree ||
verses.translation: That the double-edged dagger, smeared with blood, which hath come out (from the body of Sumbh)

verses.gurmukhi: ਜਾਣ ਰਜਾਦੀ ਉਤਰੀ ਪੈਨ ਸੂਹੀ ਸਾਰੀ ॥੫੩॥
verses.transliteration: jaan rajaadhee utaree pain soohee saaree ||53||
verses.translation: Seems like a princess coming down from her loft, wearing the red sari.53.

verses.gurmukhi: ਦੁਰਗਾ ਅਤੈ ਦਾਨਵੀ ਭੇੜ ਪਇਆ ਸਬਾਹੀਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhuragaa atai dhaanavee bheR piaa sabaahee(n) ||
verses.translation: The war between Durga and the demons started early in the morning.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸਸਤ੍ਰ ਪਜੂਤੇ ਦੁਰਗਸਾਹ ਗਹ ਸਭਨੀਂ ਬਾਹੀਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: sasatr pajoote dhuragasaeh geh sabhanee(n) baahee(n) ||
verses.translation: Durga held her weapons firmly in all her arms.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸੁੰਭ ਨਿਸੁੰਭ ਸੰਘਾਰਿਆ ਵਥ ਜੇ ਹੈ ਸਾਹੀਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: su(n)bh nisu(n)bh sa(n)ghaariaa vath je hai saahee(n) ||
verses.translation: She killed both Sumbh and Nisumbh, who were the masters of all the materials.

verses.gurmukhi: ਫਉਜਾਂ ਰਾਕਸਿਆਰੀਆਂ ਵੇਖ ਰੋਵਨਿ ਧਾਹੀਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: faujaa(n) raakasiaareeaa(n) vekh rovan dhaahee(n) ||
verses.translation: Seeing this, the helpless forces of the demons, weep bitterly.

verses.gurmukhi: ਮੁਹਿ ਕੜੂਚੇ ਘਾਹ ਦੇ ਛੱਡ ਘੋੜੇ ਰਾਹੀਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: muh kaRooche ghaeh dhe chha'dd ghoRe raahee(n) ||
verses.translation: Accepting their defeat (by putting the straws of grass in their mouth), and leaving their horses in the way

verses.gurmukhi: ਭਜਦੇ ਹੋਇ ਮਾਰੀਅਨ ਮੁੜ ਝਾਕਨ ਨਾਹੀਂ ॥੫੪॥
verses.transliteration: bhajadhe hoi maareean muR jhaakan naahee(n) ||54||
verses.translation: They are being killed, while fleeing, without looking back.54.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸੁੰਭ ਨਿਸੁੰਭ ਪਠਾਇਆ ਜਮ ਦੇ ਧਾਮ ਨੋ ॥
verses.transliteration: su(n)bh nisu(n)bh paThaiaa jam dhe dhaam no ||
verses.translation: Sumbh and Nisumbh were dispatched to the abode of Yama

verses.gurmukhi: ਇੰਦ੍ਰ ਸੱਦ ਬੁਲਾਇਆ ਰਾਜ ਅਭਿਸ਼ੇਖਨੋ ॥
verses.transliteration: ei(n)dhr sa'dh bulaiaa raaj abhishekhano ||
verses.translation: And Indra was called for crowning him.

verses.gurmukhi: ਸਿਰ ਪਰ ਛਤ੍ਰ ਫਿਰਾਇਆ ਰਾਜੇ ਇੰਦ੍ਰ ਦੈ ॥
verses.transliteration: sir par chhatr firaiaa raaje i(n)dhr dhai ||
verses.translation: The canopy was held up over the head of king Indra.

verses.gurmukhi: ਚਉਦਹ ਲੋਕਾਂ ਛਾਇਆ ਜਸੁ ਜਗਮਾਤ ਦਾ ॥
verses.transliteration: chaudheh lokaa(n) chhaiaa jas jagamaat dhaa ||
verses.translation: The praise of the mother of the universe spread over all the fourteen worlds.

verses.gurmukhi: ਦੁਰਗਾ ਪਾਠ ਬਣਾਇਆ ਸਭੇ ਪਉੜੀਆਂ ॥
verses.transliteration: dhuragaa paaTh banaiaa sabhe pauReeaa(n) ||
verses.translation: All the Pauris (stanza) of this DURGA PATH (The text about the exploits of Durga) have been composed

verses.gurmukhi: ਫੇਰ ਨ ਜੂਨੀ ਆਇਆ ਜਿਨ ਇਹ ਗਾਇਆ ॥੫੫॥
verses.transliteration: fer na joonee aaiaa jin ieh gaiaa ||55||
verses.translation: And that person who sings it, will not take birth again.55.

verses.gurmukhi: ਇਤਿ ਸ੍ਰੀ ਦੁਰਗਾ ਕੀ ਵਾਰ ਸਮਾਪਤੰ ਸਤੁ ਸੁਭਮ ਸਤੁ ॥
verses.transliteration: eit sree dhuragaa kee vaar samaapata(n) sat subham sat ||
